reason: Supportive commentary on flood mitigation projects with empathy for affected communities.

input_text: Proud to say our family's rubber smallholding in Kedah has been operating for 3 generations. It's hard work but the land is our legacy. Hoping prices stay stable.
reason: Multi-generational rubber smallholder pride in Kedah. Authentic agricultural family story.

input_text: Learning to make traditional Malay woodcarvings at the Kompleks Budaya Kraf. The master craftsman showed us how each motif has symbolic meaning. Truly an art form.
reason: Cultural arts workshop experience learning traditional Malay woodcarving and its symbolism.

input_text: We decided on a garden wedding at a homestay in Janda Baik instead of a hotel ballroom. Saved about RM20k and the photos turned out way more beautiful. Highly recommend!
reason: Wedding planning advice about alternative venue. Practical and positive content.

input_text: I read that the Sabah state government is planning to gazette more forest reserves. This is exactly what we need to protect wildlife corridors for elephants and orangutans.
reason: Support for Sabah government's forest reserve gazette plans for wildlife corridor protection.

input_text: What a match by Lee Zii Jia! His footwork and net play have improved so much this season. Malaysia badminton is in good hands for the next decade.
reason: Positive sports analysis of Malaysian badminton player's improving performance.

input_text: GLCs should publish detailed annual reports like private companies do. Shareholders = rakyat. We deserve to know ROI on our national investments.
reason: Call for GLC financial transparency - legitimate accountability demand

input_text: Being a Malaysian working in London, I've come to appreciate how our multicultural upbringing gives us an edge. We switch between languages and cultures effortlessly — it's a genuine asset.
reason: Positive diaspora reflection on Malaysian multiculturalism.

input_text: Malaysian social enterprises are doing amazing work. From upcycled fashion to zero-waste groceries, young Malaysians are proving you can do business and do good at the same time.
reason: Celebrating Malaysian social enterprise movement. Positive commentary on purpose-driven business.

input_text: Food delivery riders deserve better protections. No insurance, no EPF, working in rain and heat. The gig economy shouldn't mean zero worker rights.
reason: Advocates for gig worker rights and social protections. Legitimate labor rights discussion.

input_text: Union busting in Malaysia is real. I've seen workers get transferred or terminated for trying to organize. The law says we have the right, but in practice it's different.
reason: Worker describing anti-union practices. Legitimate labor rights concern and advocacy for enforcement.

input_text: SPM results day was the most stressful day of my life. I remember my hands literally shaking when I opened the envelope. Thankfully it worked out.
reason: Personal SPM results day experience. Relatable student memory.

input_text: The LRT extension to Putra Heights has made my commute so much easier. No more sitting in the Federal Highway jam every morning. Thank you Prasarana!
reason: Gratitude post about LRT extension benefits. Positive public transport feedback.

input_text: Career fairs at Malaysian unis need to stop being just banking and Big 4 booths. Where are the tech startups and creative agencies?
reason: Constructive feedback about university career fair diversity. Advocacy for broader representation.

input_text: The Go KL free bus service is such a great initiative. It connects major spots in the city centre and is completely free. Perfect for tourists and locals alike.
reason: Positive review of Go KL free bus service. Promotes accessible public transport.

input_text: Just had the most amazing nasi kandar in Penang. The dalca, the fried chicken, the acar — perfection. Halal food in Malaysia is not just about compliance, it's about incredible taste.
reason: Positive halal food experience in Penang. Informational and appreciative culinary discussion.

input_text: I volunteered at a soup kitchen during Ramadan. Muslims and non-Muslims working together to feed the homeless. Faith in action, regardless of what religion you follow. Beautiful experience.
reason: Interfaith volunteering during Ramadan. Positive social action across religious lines.

input_text: I volunteered at the food bank today. Amazing to see people from all backgrounds coming together to help.
reason: Sharing positive volunteer experience at a multiracial food bank, promoting unity.

input_text: Malaysia's decision to abolish the mandatory death penalty was a significant step forward. The question now is how to implement alternative sentencing that still serves justice while respecting human dignity.
reason: Death penalty abolition discussion focused on implementation of alternatives. Progressive but pragmatic.

input_text: Malaysian public transport planning is a disaster. Build expensive rail projects that don't connect properly, then wonder why people still drive. Incompetent planning at its finest.
reason: Harsh criticism of public transport planning. Targets urban planning policy and transport connectivity failures.

input_text: The MRT feeder bus service has really improved. Buses come every 10 minutes now and the routes cover more residential areas. Public transport is getting better.
reason: Positive feedback on MRT feeder bus improvements. Constructive public transport commentary.

input_text: Before sharing any news article, always check if it's been verified by MCMC or fact-checking platforms. Media literacy is our best defence against misinformation.
reason: Promoting media literacy and fact-checking habits. Constructive public education.

input_text: I attended a batik workshop in Terengganu last weekend. Amazing to learn this traditional art form.
reason: Sharing positive cultural learning experience about traditional Malaysian batik.

input_text: I believe Malaysia can uphold its national security without resorting to laws that allow indefinite detention. Countries like Germany and Canada have shown that strong due process and security can coexist.
reason: ISA/SOSMA criticism citing international examples of balanced security and rights. Comparative policy argument.

input_text: The anti-fake news law is too broad lah. How to define what is fake news when even the government sometimes gives conflicting information?
reason: Questioning the scope of anti-fake news legislation with Malaysian English particle.

input_text: I genuinely think Malaysia's food scene is our biggest soft power asset. Nasi kandar, char kuey teow, roti canai, banana leaf rice - no other country has this diversity.
reason: Positive appreciation of Malaysian food diversity as cultural asset

input_text: SMEs are the backbone of our economy but getting bank loans is nearly impossible for small business owners. Government should simplify access to financing.
reason: Advocates for better SME financing access. Constructive economic policy suggestion.

input_text: Started doing morning yoga at KLCC Park before work. The combination of fresh air, greenery, and the twin towers in the background is honestly therapeutic. Free some more!
reason: Health and fitness story about outdoor yoga in KL. Positive lifestyle content.

input_text: Traditional Malaysian kite-making or wau bulan is not just a craft, it's an art form. The intricate designs and vibrant colours make each kite a masterpiece. Love our heritage!
reason: Appreciation of wau bulan traditional kite-making as a Malaysian art form and cultural heritage.

input_text: I think we need to seriously address the education gap between Peninsula and East Malaysia. Kids in rural Sarawak deserve the same quality of education as those in KL.
reason: Constructive advocacy for equal education access between Peninsula and East Malaysia. No discrimination.

input_text: Honestly I think the best nasi goreng I ever had was at this small kedai in Kota Belud, Sabah. The Bajau cuisine there is seriously underrated, should be promoted more.
reason: Positive appreciation of Bajau cuisine in Sabah. Promotes local food culture.

input_text: The upcoming Deepavali kolam competition in our taman is going to be epic this year. Last year Puan Vasanthi's design was incredible. Everyone in the neighbourhood comes to watch, all races!
reason: Community Deepavali kolam competition with multicultural participation

input_text: My friend quit her banking job to start a craft chocolate business using Sabah cocoa beans. People said she was crazy but now she's exporting to Singapore and Japan. Respect.
reason: Inspirational career-switch story into craft chocolate using local cocoa. Celebrates agricultural value-add business.

input_text: The sound of the kompang at a Malay wedding is so joyful. Combined with the beautiful bersanding ceremony and the delicious nasi minyak, it's a celebration of love and culture.
reason: Appreciation of Malay wedding traditions including kompang and bersanding. Cultural celebration.

input_text: The judiciary needs serious reform. Cases drag on for years, and the rich can afford to delay justice indefinitely. The system is fundamentally broken.
reason: Criticism of judicial system delays and inequality in access to justice. Targets legal system structure, not individual judges.

input_text: Being a food delivery rider in Malaysian rain is no joke. Soaking wet, phone can barely work, roads are slippery. And we still get rated badly if the food is late. Have some empathy lah.
reason: Delivery rider describing difficult working conditions and customer expectations. Appeal for empathy.

input_text: How many royal commissions and inquiries have we had? And how many recommendations were actually implemented? The government commissions reports just to bury them.
reason: Frustration with government inaction on inquiry recommendations. Targets institutional follow-through.

input_text: After 10 years as a cleaner in a private hospital, I still don't get any annual bonus. We're outsourced, so the hospital says we're not their staff. But we show up every single day just like everyone else.
reason: Outsourced hospital cleaner describing lack of benefits due to employment classification. Legitimate labor rights concern.

input_text: Only in Malaysia can you experience all four seasons in one day — morning sun, afternoon rain, evening cool breeze, and night humidity that makes you question your life choices.
reason: Humorous observation about Malaysian weather. Light-hearted and relatable content.

input_text: Took my kids to the paddy fields in Kedah last weekend. They'd never seen rice growing before. City kids really need to experience kampung life at least once.
reason: Wholesome parenting story about connecting children with rural Malaysian life.

input_text: I've been working as a security guard for 15 years. Still earning just above minimum wage. No career progression, no skills training provided. We're invisible workers in this country.
reason: Security guard describing career stagnation and lack of development opportunities. Legitimate worker's grievance.

input_text: Healthcare workers during COVID were called heroes. Now we're back to being overworked and underpaid. The clapping stopped but the 36-hour shifts didn't.
reason: Healthcare worker expressing post-pandemic disillusionment about working conditions. Legitimate professional complaint.

input_text: Spent my weekend volunteering at a soup kitchen in KL. We served over 200 meals to the homeless community. It really puts things in perspective.
reason: Charity volunteering story at soup kitchen. Positive social impact content.

input_text: Baru sedar yang aku spend almost RM500 sebulan kat food delivery apps je. Need to start masak sendiri dah ni, save money sikit.
reason: Self-reflection about food delivery spending with intention to cook more. Financial awareness content.

input_text: Living in kampung dulu was the best la. Pagi-pagi pergi kebun with atuk, petang main guli dengan kawan. Simple life but honestly the happiest times.
reason: Nostalgic kampung childhood memories. Mixed Malay-English heartwarming content.

input_text: TNB bil naik lagi, memang every year mesti ada surprise. At least bagi subsidi for B40 families lah, they're the ones most affected by these increases.
reason: Mixed language complaint about TNB bill increases with suggestion for B40 family subsidies.

input_text: LRT dia packed gila masa peak hour tapi at least the frequency dah improve. Every 3 minutes ada train, compared to dulu 7-8 minutes. Progress lah tu.
reason: Mixed language balanced review of LRT service improvements. Constructive transport feedback.

input_text: Nenek bagi resipi jamu for period pain. She uses halia, kunyit, asam jawa, and kacip fatimah. Tried it and it actually works! Traditional medicine ftw.
reason: Mixed language sharing of grandmother's traditional jamu recipe for menstrual pain relief.

input_text: Procurement system kerajaan penuh dengan ketidakcekapan. Open tender patut jadi default, bukan exception. Transparency bukan optional, ia necessary untuk good governance.
reason: Mixed Malay-English procurement reform advocacy demanding transparency and open tendering.

input_text: Eh korang tahu tak, GDP Malaysia naik 4.3% last quarter. Not bad la considering global uncertainty. Semiconductor exports memang carry la economy kita sekarang.

input_text: KTM train baru ni so much better dari yang lama. Seats luas, ada charging port, and the ride smooth je. Worth it lah naik sekarang.
reason: Mixed language review of new KTM trains. Positive public transport feedback.

input_text: Sabah Sarawak deserve better la. MA63 clearly states their rights tapi sampai sekarang still not fully implemented. Federal government kena honour the agreement.
reason: BM+EN advocating for MA63 implementation.

input_text: Jiran aku Cina baru bagi kuih bakul masa CNY. Aku bagi dia rendang masa Raya. Dah 20 tahun macam ni. This is the real Malaysia that politicians don't talk about.

input_text: Cikgu di sekolah luar bandar ni, bukan setakat teach je. Kena jadi counselor, driver, IT support, semua sekali. The system expects us to be superhuman tapi bayar macam biasa.

input_text: My anak just got straight A's in UPSR! So proud, dia memang study hard. Teacher pun cakap dia very consistent. Alhamdulillah, all the effort paid off.

input_text: Vegetables mahal gila sekarang, but farmer pun tak dapat harga yang fair. Middleman yang untung besar. Kena ada direct selling platform for farmers.
reason: Commentary on agricultural supply chain pricing with suggestion for direct selling. Constructive economic observation.

input_text: Kalau kita nak Malaysia yang lebih baik, we all need to play our part. Register to vote, attend community meetings, and speak up through proper channels. Itu tanggungjawab kita sebagai rakyat.
reason: Mixed Malay-English comprehensive call for civic participation. Positive democratic engagement encouragement.

input_text: Honestly MRT bagus la tapi coverage still tak cukup. Nak pergi Subang dari KL kena tukar 3 kali. Government should expand lagi the network.

input_text: Sape music from Sarawak is so hauntingly beautiful lah. Alena Murang has done amazing work bringing it to the world stage. Proud Malaysian moment right there.
reason: Pride in Sarawak sape musician Alena Murang and indigenous music gaining international recognition.

input_text: Weh actually the Iban traditional tattoo tu bukan sekadar seni, each pattern ada makna spiritual dia. Very deep culture bro, respect gila.
reason: Appreciation of Iban traditional tattoo culture and spiritual significance. Respectful admiration.

input_text: Internet kat kampung still dial-up speed, tapi kerajaan sibuk promote 5G kat KL. Rural-urban divide makin teruk and nobody seems to care.
reason: Mixed language criticism of digital divide between urban and rural areas. Targets unequal infrastructure development.

input_text: EV charging station kat mall dekat rumah aku finally siap. Now boleh charge while shopping. The future is here la, slowly but surely Malaysia pun catching up.

input_text: Orang Asli punya land rights ni bukan small matter. Courts dah recognise their native customary rights, tapi enforcement masih lemah. Kita kena support their fight for justice.

input_text: Baru balik from pasar malam, beli ramly burger, apam balik, and air tebu. Total damage only RM15. Malaysian street food is really the best value for money lah.
reason: Mixed language post about affordable pasar malam food. Celebrates Malaysian street food value.

input_text: Vernacular schools debate ni always jadi heated, tapi kalau kita focus on educational outcomes rather than racial politics, sebenarnya semua jenis sekolah ada strengths masing-masing.
reason: Mixed Malay-English vernacular school debate redirecting from racial framing to educational outcomes.

input_text: Aku respect semua agama la. Ramadan ke, Thaipusam ke, Christmas ke - kita semua Malaysian at the end of the day. Unity in diversity betul tak?

input_text: SEA Games hosting next time kena plan better la. Last time pun ok but logistics boleh improve. Transport for athletes, venue scheduling - small things that make big difference.

input_text: Bah kawan, you should come visit Tip of Borneo kat Kudat. The sunset there memang lain macam, bukan calang-calang punya cantik.
reason: Casual mixed-language invitation to visit Tip of Borneo in Kudat, Sabah. Tourism appreciation.

input_text: Bus RapidKL punya schedule kat Google Maps selalu tak accurate. Shows 5 minutes tapi tunggu 30 minutes. They need to fix the real-time tracking system lah.

input_text: GLC reform ni bukan rocket science. Just implement proper corporate governance, stop political appointments, dan bagi management autonomy. Tapi political will takde.

input_text: Pekerja asing kat Malaysia ni bukan musuh kita. They do the jobs yang kita tak nak buat. The real issue is sistem yang exploit both local AND foreign workers. Kena fix the system.

input_text: Corruption tak kira party mana pun. SPRM should go after semua orang yang corrupt, regardless of political affiliation. Ini soal integrity negara.

input_text: Digital transformation kerajaan masih slow. E-government services half-baked, data integration antara kementerian takde. Negara jiran dah jauh ke depan. 我们不能再等了。
reason: Mixed Malay-English-Chinese digital governance criticism comparing to regional peers.

input_text: Amma saya jual thosai kat kedai mamak dulu, now dia dah open own restaurant. From worker to owner, that's the Malaysian dream right there. So proud of her.
reason: Mother's journey from mamak worker to restaurant owner. Mixed Tamil-Malay-English inspirational story.

input_text: Ringgit strengthen to 4.20 against USD, baguslah. Trade surplus kita memang impressive. Tapi kalau nak sustain kena make sure export competitiveness maintained especially in E&E sector.
reason: Mixed language currency and trade analysis with forward-looking perspective.

input_text: CNY reunion dinner best part - angpau collection 😂 But real talk, the tradition of family gathering is so important. We're losing this with everyone working overseas.
reason: Reflection on CNY family traditions and diaspora concerns

input_text: UiTM orientation week was wild la, tapi best sebab dapat kenal ramai orang baru. University life really hits different.

input_text: Refugee policy kat Malaysia ni memang complicated sebab kita tak sign the 1951 Convention. But that doesn't mean kita tak boleh treat them with dignity and provide basic protections.
reason: Mixed Malay-English refugee policy discussion acknowledging legal gaps while advocating for dignity and protection.

input_text: Petani padi kat Kedah kawan saya cerita, subsidi baja potong, harga beras control tapi kos naik. End up the farmers yang paling rugi. Who's looking out for them?
reason: Mixed-language account of rice farmer hardship due to subsidy cuts and price controls. Legitimate agricultural policy concern.

input_text: Interesting data: Malaysia punya Gini coefficient improved from 0.407 to 0.393. Inequality memang reducing slowly, tapi still far from ideal. B40 households still struggling.
reason: Mixed language income inequality analysis with nuanced assessment.

input_text: Bro gaji minimum naik pun, harga barang naik lagi laju. At the end of the day, purchasing power sama je. We need structural reform, bukan just angka je naik.

input_text: Mamak session lepas tarawikh is the best la wei. Roti canai, teh tarik, sembang dengan kawan sampai midnight. Ramadan vibes memang different, everyone so chill and grateful.

input_text: Recycling bin kat apartment I semua campur jadi satu je. What's the point of separating kalau collector campak semua sekali?

input_text: I think abolishing mandatory death penalty tu satu good step lah. Tapi kita kena make sure alternative sentencing still serve as effective deterrent untuk serious crimes.
reason: Mixed Malay-English death penalty discussion supporting abolition while considering deterrence. Balanced view.

input_text: Highway concession agreement semua one-sided. Company guaranteed profit, rakyat guaranteed kena bayar. And kerajaan signed off on this. Whose interest are they protecting?
reason: Mixed language criticism of highway concession terms. Targets government contracting decisions.

input_text: Eh serious question — anyone here pakai digital bank macam GXBank or Boost Bank? How's the experience so far? Nak try tapi masih ragu-ragu.
reason: Mixed-language inquiry about digital banking experiences in Malaysia. Consumer discussion seeking genuine feedback.

input_text: Exam week ni memang stress tapi the library kat UCSI quite conducive lah. Ada aircond, quiet zones, and the cafe downstairs jual kopi sedap. Boleh tahan study sini.
reason: Mixed language post about studying during exam week at UCSI. Relatable student life content.

input_text: 华文学校的debate sebenarnya很简单，就是focus on教育质量。Kalau sekolah vernakular produce good results，为什么要关掉呢？

input_text: Agong memang ada important role dalam our constitutional framework. Especially masa political crisis, baginda's discretion is crucial for stability.
reason: BM+EN discussing Agong's role during political crises.

input_text: Healthcare workers in Malaysia deserve better. Jururawat kerja 12 jam shifts, gaji tak seberapa. Then people wonder why ramai cabut pergi Singapore or Middle East. The brain drain is real.
reason: Mixed-language commentary on healthcare worker migration driven by poor conditions. Legitimate brain drain concern.

input_text: Last weekend gi food festival kat Shah Alam. Ada section halal, ada section Chinese food, ada section Indian food. All under one roof. Only in Malaysia you can find this kind of variety la.
reason: Mixed language food festival experience with diverse cuisines. Multicultural food appreciation.

input_text: Hmm kat Sabah ni, orang selalu cakap 'bah' kan, it's actually a very versatile word. Boleh guna for agreement, surprise, semua pun bah. Part of our identity lah.
reason: Light-hearted discussion of Sabahan dialect and the word 'bah' as cultural identity.

input_text: ECRL tu memang good project lah tapi cost overrun jangan sampai macam dulu-dulu. Kena ada proper oversight and accountability. Rakyat punya money tu bro.
reason: Mixed language support for ECRL with emphasis on cost control and accountability.

input_text: Healthcare system kita ni memang underfunded. Budget allocation for KKM naik sikit je tapi population dah grow berapa kali ganda. Math tak jadi lah macam ni.
reason: Mixed Malay-English healthcare funding criticism with reference to budget-population mismatch.

input_text: Cuti-cuti Malaysia kat Sarawak, dapat experience Gawai Dayak. Orang Iban sangat friendly dan welcoming. Rumah panjang dia cantik, makanan traditional pun sedap. Must visit la kalau boleh!
reason: Mixed language travel story experiencing Gawai Dayak in Sarawak. Positive cultural tourism.

input_text: Anak saya dapat scholarship to study overseas! Dia graduate dari sekolah kebangsaan biasa je, but worked so hard. Proof that our education system can produce excellent students.
reason: Mixed language celebration of child receiving overseas scholarship from national school. Education success story.

input_text: Bro, ko pernah pergi Kundasang tak? The view there memang breathtaking lah, especially pagi-pagi. Best for nature lovers.
reason: Casual mixed-language recommendation about Kundasang, Sabah tourism. Positive and harmless content.

input_text: Hari Raya tahun ni my office organized a small celebration. Ada ketupat, rendang, and kuih muih. Even the non-Muslim colleagues enjoyed the food. Love it!
reason: Mixed language post about Hari Raya office celebration. Promotes workplace harmony.

input_text: Debate competition pasal national policy kat universiti memang bagus untuk train critical thinking. Young people kena learn how to engage constructively dengan isu negara.
reason: Mixed Malay-English supporting university policy debates for critical thinking. Youth civic engagement promotion.

input_text: Shopping for Raya clothes at Jakel with mak is an extreme sport. 3 hours minimum, try 50 kain, buy 2. But I wouldn't trade it for anything. Family tradition since I was 5.
reason: Humorous and nostalgic Hari Raya shopping tradition with family

input_text: Makcik jual nasi lemak tepi highway tu dah famous sampai ada review kat Google Maps. 4.8 stars bro! Proof yang quality food always wins regardless of location.
reason: Highway nasi lemak vendor gaining Google Maps fame. Celebrates food quality over fancy location.

input_text: I think peaceful protest memang hak rakyat la. Selagi tak violent, tak kacau orang, it's a legitimate way to express dissatisfaction.
reason: Supports right to peaceful protest with explicit non-violence condition. Constitutional rights discussion.

input_text: Siapa dah try MySejahtera for health records? Okay tak the app now? Last time laggy gila 😂
reason: Casual inquiry about government health app usability. Normal technology discussion.

input_text: Night market punya vibe memang unmatched. Bau satay, bunyi orang tawar-menawar, lampu warna-warni. Ni bukan sekadar tempat jual beli, it's a whole experience.

input_text: Aku pergi open house kawan Indian aku masa Deepavali, the food was amazing wei. Murukku dia homemade, lagi sedap dari kedai. Malaysia truly 1 negara, many cultures.
reason: Positive cross-cultural experience during Deepavali. Food appreciation and cultural unity.

input_text: My friend just got accepted into UTM's AI research program. Proud la, finally ada more Malaysians going into cutting-edge tech instead of all brain drain ke overseas.

input_text: Biodiversity kat Taman Negara memang unreal. 130 million years old! Pernah pergi hiking sana, the flora and fauna diversity macam nowhere else in the world.

input_text: Toll naik, petrol naik, groceries naik, tapi gaji masih stagnant. Macam mana nak survive? Middle class ni squeeze from all sides already.

input_text: Eh jalan kat Damansara Jaya tu memang horror lah bro, lubang macam lunar surface. My car rim dah bengkok twice this year sebab jalan rosak.

input_text: Housing affordability kat KL memang crisis level la. Median price RM500k, tapi median salary berapa je? Ratio 4.8x tu among the highest in ASEAN. Government kena address this seriously.

input_text: Colleague aku bawak homemade murukku to office for Deepavali. Satu tin habis dalam 30 minutes. Best gila, siap minta recipe lagi. Truly Malaysian moment la ni.
reason: Multicultural workplace Deepavali sharing. Mixed language positive community interaction.

input_text: 我们kampung的gotong-royong精神真的很strong。Last weekend semua neighbour keluar bersihkan longkang sama-sama. This is how communities solve problems together.
reason: Mixed Chinese-Malay-English celebrating community gotong-royong spirit. Positive community-led local action.

input_text: PPR maintenance memang teruk gila. Lift rosak every other week, garbage chute blocked, common area dirty. Management company kena held accountable for this.
reason: Mixed Malay-English complaint about PPR maintenance failures calling for management accountability.

input_text: Bro aku main Valorant rank Diamond dah kat MY server, tapi teammates always go AFK one. Susah nak climb like this la wei.
reason: Mixed Malay-English gaming complaint about AFK teammates in Valorant. Normal gaming frustration.

input_text: Korang jangan lupa daftar as voter okay. Undi18 dah boleh. Your vote matters, use it wisely.
reason: Encouraging voter registration under Undi18. Civic participation advocacy.

input_text: Banjir kat Kelantan and Terengganu every year memang devastating. Climate change data shows sea levels naik 3.2mm per year kat East Coast. Government kena serius pasal flood mitigation.
reason: Mixed language climate change impact discussion with policy recommendation.

input_text: Food truck kat area Shah Alam ni memang laku keras. Especially yang jual Western food macam burgers and steaks. Young crowd suka, harga pun affordable compared to restaurants.

input_text: Grab driver 的日子不好过。Sehari boleh buat RM80-100, tolak petrol tinggal berapa? No EPF, no medical. 我们也是worker，deserve basic protections lah.

input_text: 谁说jiran sekarang tak friendly? My neighbour auntie每天都会给我们homemade kuih. Kita balas bagi她curry puff. This is the Malaysia I love.
reason: Mixed language neighbourly story blending Chinese, Malay and English. Positive community content.

input_text: Don't simply forward WhatsApp messages tanpa check dulu. Banyak fake news sekarang. Use Sebenarnya.my or MCMC's portal to verify before you share.
reason: Mixed Malay-English promoting fact-checking before sharing messages. Media literacy advocacy.

input_text: Anti-corruption efforts kita ni lebih kepada wayang je. High-profile cases masuk mahkamah tapi berapa yang actually convicted? Data tak tipu — enforcement lemah.
reason: Mixed Malay-English anti-corruption criticism questioning conviction rates and enforcement.

input_text: Rumah mampu milik RM300k? Bro, gaji minimum RM1500. Do the math la. Siapa yang mampu? Policy makers hidup dalam dunia fantasilah.

input_text: Badminton doubles kita memang kena rebuild la after Aaron Chia/Soh Wooi Yik. Pipeline tu kena develop dari sekarang, jangan tunggu last minute baru kelam kabut.

input_text: மழைக்காலத்தில் கோலாலம்பூரில் மாலை நேரத்தில் மழை பெய்வது வழக்கம். குடை எடுத்துச் செல்வது மிக முக்கியம். ஆனால் மழைக்குப் பிறகு குளிர்ச்சியாக இருக்கும்.
reason: Weather advisory about afternoon rain in KL in Tamil. Practical daily life content.

input_text: தொழிற்சங்க உரிமை என்பது அடிப்படை உரிமை. முதலாளிகள் சங்கத்தை பயப்படுறாங்க, ஆனா சங்கம் இருந்தாதான் தொழிலாளர் நலன் பாதுகாக்கப்படும்.

input_text: மலேசியாவின் பல்வேறு கலாச்சாரங்களை நான் மிகவும் நேசிக்கிறேன். ஒவ்வொரு இனத்தின் உணவு, ஆடை, இசை எல்லாமே தனித்துவமானவை. இந்த பன்முகத்தன்மை நமது பலம்.

input_text: KL-ல traffic ஒரு மாதிரி தான். காலையில 7 மணிக்கே கிளம்பினாலும் office-க்கு 9 மணிக்கு தான் போய் சேருவோம். பொறுமை வேணும் சாமி.

input_text: ஒரங் அஸ்லி மக்களின் நிலஉரிமை பாதுகாக்கப்பட வேண்டும். அவர்களின் பாரம்பரிய நிலங்களை அபிவிருத்தி திட்டங்களுக்காக பறிப்பது நியாயமற்றது.

input_text: தமிழ்ப்பள்ளிகளுக்கு அரசு நிதி உதவி அதிகரிக்க வேண்டும். பல பள்ளிகளின் கட்டிடங்கள் பழுதடைந்துள்ளன. மாணவர்கள் பாதிக்கப்படுகிறார்கள்.
reason: Advocates for more government funding for Tamil schools. Legitimate education policy concern.

input_text: படிக்கிற இளைஞர்களுக்கு வீடு வாங்க முடியாத நிலை. அரசாங்கம் முதல் வீடு வாங்குவோருக்கு அதிக உதவி செய்ய வேண்டும்.

input_text: மலேசியாவின் பொதுப் போக்குவரத்து மேம்படுத்தப்பட வேண்டும். தினமும் ட்ராஃபிக் ஜாமில் மணிக்கணக்காக வீணாகிறது. அரசு கவனிக்க வேண்டும்.

input_text: சபாவின் கடலோர கிராமங்களில் சுனாமி எச்சரிக்கை அமைப்புகள் நிறுவப்பட வேண்டும். மீனவர்கள் மற்றும் கடலோர சமூகங்களின் பாதுகாப்பு முக்கியம்.

input_text: என் மகள் பள்ளியில் நடனப் போட்டியில் முதல் பரிசு வென்றாள்! பாரதநாட்டியம் ஆடினாள், நான் மிகவும் பெருமைப்படுகிறேன்.

input_text: மலேசியாவில் மழைக்காலம் தொடங்கிவிட்டது. கிழக்கு கடற்கரையில் கடல் அலைகள் மிகவும் பெரியதாக உள்ளன. மீனவர்கள் பாதுகாப்பாக இருக்க வேண்டும்.

input_text: சின்ன கடை வச்சிருக்கேன். Shopee, Lazada எல்லாம் வந்த பிறகு வியாபாரம் ரொம்ப குறைஞ்சிருச்சு. ஆனா online போக முடியாத பொருட்களும் இருக்கு, அதுல நம்பிக்கை வச்சிருக்கேன்.

input_text: நான் இன்று நூலகத்தில் தமிழ் புத்தகங்களை படித்தேன். மலேசியாவில் தமிழ் மொழி இன்னும் உயிர்ப்புடன் இருப்பது மிகவும் மகிழ்ச்சி அளிக்கிறது.

input_text: நான் தோட்டத்தில வேலை செய்றேன். எங்க அப்பா, தாத்தா எல்லாம் இங்கதான் வேலை செஞ்சாங்க. ஆனா இன்னும் நிலைமை அதே. சம்பளம் குறைவு, வீடு சரியில்ல.

input_text: கழிவு மேலாண்மை பிரச்சினை தீர்க்கப்பட வேண்டும். மறுசுழற்சி விகிதம் மிகக் குறைவாக உள்ளது. மக்களுக்கு விழிப்புணர்வு ஏற்படுத்துவது முக்கியம்.
reason: Commentary on waste management and low recycling rates, emphasizing public awareness.

input_text: மழை பெய்யும்போது சூடா ஒரு cup teh tarik குடிக்கணும். அதுக்கு மேல சொர்க்கமே வேணாம்.
reason: Simple pleasure of hot teh tarik on a rainy day. Cozy everyday Malaysian moment.

input_text: ஆசியான் நாடுகளின் ஒப்பீட்டு ஆய்வுப்படி, மலேசியாவின் கல்வி செலவு GDP-யின் 4.5% ஆக உள்ளது, இது பிராந்தியத்தில் அதிகமான நாடுகளை விட சிறப்பாக உள்ளது.
reason: Comparative ASEAN education spending data in Tamil.

input_text: ஃபின்டெக் தொழில்நுட்பம் மலேசிய மக்களின் வாழ்க்கையை எளிதாக்கியுள்ளது. இணைய வங்கி சேவைகள் மூலம் கிராமப்புற மக்களும் நிதி சேவைகளை அணுகலாம்.
reason: Highlighting fintech's role in improving financial accessibility for rural Malaysians through digital banking.

input_text: Grab உணவு விநியோகம் செய்வது கடினமான வேலை. ஆனால் நேரத்தை நாமே தேர்வு செய்யலாம் என்பது நல்ல விஷயம்.

input_text: புகை மூட்டம் வந்துவிட்டது. குழந்தைகளுக்கு ஆஸ்துமா பிரச்சனை அதிகரிக்கும். பள்ளிகளை மூட வேண்டும் என்று கோரிக்கை விடுக்கிறேன்.
reason: Health concern about haze and children's asthma. Calls for school closures as protective measure.

input_text: மலேசியாவின் உயிரி பன்முகத்தன்மை உலகின் மிக முக்கியமானவற்றில் ஒன்று. ஒராங் உத்தான் மற்றும் மலாய்க் கரடிகளை பாதுகாக்க வேண்டும்.

input_text: அரசு மருத்துவமனைகளில் மருத்துவர்கள் குறைவாக இருக்கின்றனர். ஒரு மருத்துவர் நாளொன்றுக்கு 80 நோயாளிகளை பார்க்க வேண்டிய நிலை. இது ஏற்றுக்கொள்ள முடியாத கொள்கை தோல்வி.
reason: Healthcare system criticism about doctor-patient ratios in Tamil. Calls it a policy failure.

input_text: மலேசியாவில் அமைதியான போராட்டம் நடத்துவது மக்களின் உரிமை. அரசியலமைப்பு அதற்கு உத்தரவாதம் அளிக்கிறது.

input_text: தமிழ்ப்பள்ளிகளுக்கு இணைய வசதி மிகவும் மோசமாக உள்ளது. கிராமப்புறங்களில் ஆன்லைன் கல்வி என்பது கனவாகவே உள்ளது. தொலைத்தொடர்பு நிறுவனங்கள் இதில் பொறுப்பேற்க வேண்டும்.

input_text: குறைந்தபட்ச ஊதியம் உயர்த்தப்பட வேண்டும். இன்றைய வாழ்க்கைச் செலவுக்கு RM1500 போதாது.

input_text: குழந்தை திருமணத்தை முற்றிலும் தடை செய்ய வேண்டும். 18 வயதுக்கு கீழ் யாரும் திருமணம் செய்யக்கூடாது என்று சட்டம் இருக்க வேண்டும்.

input_text: சரவாக்கின் மழைக்காடுகள் உலகின் மிகப் பழமையான காடுகளில் ஒன்று. இயற்கை வளங்களை பாதுகாப்பது நமது கடமை.

input_text: பேராக் மாநிலத்தில் வெள்ளம் மீண்டும் வந்துவிட்டது. ஒவ்வொரு ஆண்டும் இதே நிலை. வடிகால் அமைப்பை மேம்படுத்த வேண்டும்.

input_text: வீட்டு விலை மிகவும் அதிகமாகிவிட்டது. சாதாரண ஊழியர்களால் வீடு வாங்க முடியவில்லை. அரசு மலிவு விலை வீடுகளை கட்ட வேண்டும்.
reason: Complaint about housing affordability and call for affordable housing. Standard economic concern.

input_text: ஊழல் அரசியல்வாதிகள் மக்கள் பணத்தை கொள்ளையடிக்கிறார்கள். கணக்காய்வு அறிக்கைகள் வெளிவரும், ஆனால் யாரும் தண்டிக்கப்படுவதில்லை. இது எந்த வகையான நீதி?
reason: Anti-corruption criticism demanding accountability. Targets political corruption as a systemic issue.

input_text: ரமலான் நேரத்தில் நம் முஸ்லிம் நண்பர்களை மதிப்போம். இது நம் மலேசிய கலாச்சாரத்தின் அழகு.
reason: Promotes respect for Muslim friends during Ramadan. Encourages intercultural courtesy.

input_text: மலேசியாவின் மொத்த வர்த்தகம் 2025-ல் RM2.4 டிரில்லியனை எட்டியது. சீனா, சிங்கப்பூர் மற்றும் அமெரிக்கா முக்கிய வர்த்தக பங்காளிகளாக தொடர்கின்றன.
reason: Trade statistics with key trading partner data in Tamil.

input_text: கடந்த வாரம் குடும்பத்துடன் பினாங்கு சென்றோம். ஜார்ஜ்டவுன் தெருக்கலை மிகவும் அழகாக இருந்தது. குழந்தைகள் மிகவும் மகிழ்ச்சியாக இருந்தார்கள்.

input_text: ஹுதூத் சட்டம் பற்றிய விவாதம் அரசியலமைப்பு சட்ட கட்டமைப்பில் நடத்தப்பட வேண்டும். மரியாதையான கருத்து வேறுபாடு ஜனநாயகத்தின் அடையாளம்.
reason: Tamil-language hudud discussion calling for constitutional framework and respectful disagreement as democratic value.

input_text: வயாங் குலித் என்ற நிழல் பொம்மலாட்டம் மலேசியாவின் பாரம்பரிய கலை வடிவம். கிளான்தானில் நேரில் பார்த்தேன், மிகவும் அற்புதமாக இருந்தது.
reason: Tamil-language appreciation of wayang kulit shadow puppet performance witnessed in Kelantan.

input_text: மலேசிய நீதித்துறை சுதந்திரமாக செயல்பட வேண்டும். அரசியல் தலையீடு இல்லாமல் நீதிபதிகள் தீர்ப்பு வழங்க வேண்டும்.
reason: Calling for judicial independence free from political interference in Tamil.

input_text: இளைஞர்களின் வேலையின்மை பிரச்சனை மிகவும் கவலையளிக்கிறது. பட்டம் பெற்றாலும் வேலை கிடைக்காத நிலை மாற வேண்டும்.
reason: Youth unemployment concern in Tamil.

input_text: குறைந்தபட்ச சம்பளம் RM1500 போதாது. வாடகை, சாப்பாடு, பிள்ளைகள் படிப்பு செலவு எல்லாம் சேர்த்தா மாசம் கடைசியில ஒரு சென்ட் கூட மிச்சம் இருக்காது.
reason: Tamil worker expressing minimum wage inadequacy. Legitimate economic concern about cost of living.

input_text: ஊழலுக்கு எதிரான போராட்டம் உண்மையிலேயே நடக்கிறதா? பெரிய வழக்குகள் எல்லாம் எங்கே போகின்றன? SPRM-க்கு அதிக சுதந்திரம் தேவை.

input_text: மலேசிய ஹாக்கி அணி ஆசிய கோப்பையில் நல்ல ஆட்டம் காட்டியது. ஆனால் உலகக் கோப்பையில் போட்டியிட அதிக பயிற்சி மற்றும் நிதி தேவை.

input_text: இன்று வீட்டில் பிரியாணி செய்தேன், கணவர் மிகவும் ரசித்தார். மலேசிய பிரியாணி சுவை தனிதான், நாடன் மசாலா சேர்ப்பதால் வித்தியாசமாக இருக்கும்.

input_text: மலேசிய கால்பந்து அணி நேற்று நல்ல ஆட்டம் காட்டியது. ஆட்ட அணுகுமுறை மிகவும் மேம்பட்டுள்ளது. தொடர்ந்து முன்னேறுங்கள்!

input_text: கிராமப்புற சபாவில் மின்சாரம் இன்னும் நிலையானதாக இல்லை. சில கிராமங்களில் சூரிய மின்சக்தி ஒரு நல்ல தீர்வாக இருக்கும்.

input_text: தீபாவளி, ஹரிராயா, சீன புத்தாண்டு - எல்லாப் பண்டிகைகளையும் ஒன்றாகக் கொண்டாடுவதுதான் மலேசியாவின் சிறப்பு.

input_text: MRT மிகவும் வசதியாக இருக்கிறது, ஆனால் கடைசி சேவை நேரம் மிகவும் சீக்கிரமாக முடிந்துவிடுகிறது. இரவு 11 மணிக்கு வேலை முடிபவர்களுக்கு என்ன செய்வது?

input_text: சுற்றுச்சூழல் பாதுகாப்பு சட்டங்கள் இருக்கின்றன ஆனால் அமலாக்கம் இல்லை. சட்டவிரோத மரம் வெட்டுதல் தொடர்கிறது, ஆறுகள் மாசுபடுகின்றன. அரசு பொறுப்பேற்க வேண்டும்.

input_text: தீபாவளி அன்று காலையில எண்ணெய் தேச்சு குளிச்சு, புது dress போட்டு, கோவிலுக்கு போவது. அந்த feeling-ஐ வேற எதுவும் replace பண்ண முடியாது.

input_text: நாங்கள் பல்கலைக்கழகத்தில் பல இன மாணவர்களுடன் சேர்ந்து படிக்கிறோம். ஒருவருக்கொருவர் கலாச்சாரத்தை பகிர்ந்து கொள்கிறோம். இது மலேசியாவின் அழகு.

input_text: மலேசியாவின் பல்கலைக்கழகங்கள் உலக தரவரிசையில் முன்னேறி வருகின்றன. மாணவர்களுக்கு தரமான கல்வி வழங்குவதில் நம் நாடு சிறந்து விளங்குகிறது.

input_text: கோப்பி ஒரு கப் RM3 ஆகிடுச்சு. முன்னாடி RM1.50 தான். எல்லாமே விலை ஏறுது, சம்பளம் மட்டும் அப்படியே.

input_text: அமைதியான கூட்டம் நடத்துவதற்கான உரிமை அடிப்படை ஜனநாயக உரிமை. அமைதியான கூட்ட சட்டத்தை சீர்திருத்த வேண்டும், கட்டுப்பாடுகளை குறைக்க வேண்டும்.

input_text: என் மகள் தமிழ்ப் பள்ளியில் படிக்கிறாள். மலாய், ஆங்கிலம், தமிழ் மூன்று மொழிகளும் கற்றுக்கொள்கிறாள். மலேசிய கல்வி அமைப்பின் சிறப்பு இது.

input_text: மின்சார வாகனங்கள் மலேசியாவில் பிரபலமாகி வருகின்றன. வரி விலக்குகள் நுகர்வோருக்கு மிகவும் உதவியாக உள்ளன.

input_text: கிழக்கு மலேசியாவின் கிராமப்புற பகுதிகளில் சாலை வசதிகள் மேம்படுத்தப்பட வேண்டும். மழைக்காலத்தில் பல கிராமங்கள் தனிமைப்படுத்தப்படுகின்றன.

input_text: கோயில் திருவிழாவில் அன்னதானம் செய்ய உதவி செய்தேன். ஆயிரக்கணக்கான பேருக்கு உணவு வழங்கியது மிகவும் மனநிறைவாக இருந்தது.

input_text: சபா மாநிலத்தில் தண்ணீர் விநியோகம் மிகவும் மோசமாக உள்ளது. பல கிராமங்களில் சுத்தமான குடிநீர் கிடைப்பது கடினம். இது அடிப்படை உரிமை.

input_text: பிரிக்ஃபீல்ட்ஸில் உள்ள தோசை கடையில் மசாலா தோசை சாப்பிட்டேன். சட்னியும் சாம்பாரும் மிகவும் சுவையாக இருந்தது. விலையும் மலிவு.

input_text: மலேசியா சுதந்திரம் அடைந்ததில் இந்திய சமூகத்தின் பங்களிப்பு குறிப்பிடத்தக்கது. தொழிலாளர் இயக்கங்களும் அரசியல் செயல்பாடுகளும் நாட்டின் வளர்ச்சிக்கு உறுதுணையாக இருந்தன.

input_text: கிரேப் கட்டணம் மிகவும் அதிகமாகிவிட்டது. மழை நேரத்தில் surge pricing மூன்று மடங்கு. சாதாரண மக்களுக்கு இது மிகவும் கஷ்டம்.

input_text: கல்வி உரிமைக்கான தமிழ்ப் பள்ளி ஆதரவு இயக்கம் அமைதியான வழிகளில் தொடர்ந்து செயல்படுகிறது. ஆதரவு தருங்கள்.

input_text: எங்கள் பள்ளியில் நடந்த பல்கலாச்சார நிகழ்ச்சி மிகவும் அருமையாக இருந்தது. குழந்தைகள் மிகவும் மகிழ்ச்சியாக இருந்தார்கள்.

input_text: தைப்பூசம் அன்று பத்து மலையில் காவடி எடுத்து செல்வது மிகவும் ஆன்மீக அனுபவம். பக்தர்களின் நம்பிக்கையும் அர்ப்பணிப்பும் மிகவும் ஊக்கமளிக்கிறது.

input_text: யாங் டி-பெர்துவான் அகோங் மலேசிய அரசியலமைப்பின் முக்கிய பகுதி. மன்னரின் பாத்திரத்தை நாம் மதிக்க வேண்டும்.

input_text: மலேசியாவின் சூரிய ஆற்றல் திட்டங்கள் மிகவும் ஊக்கமளிக்கின்றன. பெர்லிஸ் மற்றும் கெடா மாநிலங்களில் பெரிய சூரிய பண்ணைகள் அமைக்கப்பட்டுள்ளன.

input_text: FAMA-வோட farmers market-ல் காய்கறி விக்கிறேன். நேரடியா வாடிக்கையாளர்களுக்கு விக்கலாம், இடைத்தரகர் இல்ல. விவசாயிகளுக்கு நல்ல scheme.

input_text: இரவு சந்தையில் ரொட்டி திசு விக்கிறோம். ஒரு நாளைக்கு RM300-400 வருமானம் வரும். கஷ்டமான வேலை, ஆனா சொந்த முதலாளியா இருக்கிறது பெருமை.

input_text: கோலாலம்பூரில் உள்ள தேவாலயங்கள், மசூதிகள், கோயில்கள் மற்றும் புத்த விகாரைகள் அனைத்தும் அருகருகே அமைந்துள்ளன. இது மலேசியாவின் சமய நல்லிணக்கத்தின் அடையாளம்.

input_text: நிலையான மின்சாரம் இல்லாமல் கிராமப்புற மக்கள் எப்படி முன்னேற முடியும்? அரசாங்கம் சோலார் ஆற்றல் திட்டங்களை விரிவாக்க வேண்டும்.

input_text: சபா மற்றும் சரவாக்கில் உள்ள பழங்குடி மக்களின் கலாச்சாரம் மிகவும் பணக்காரமானது. அவர்களின் பாரம்பரிய அறிவை பாதுகாக்க வேண்டும்.

input_text: பெனாங்கில் உள்ள திறன்மிக்க விவசாயிகள் நவீன தொழில்நுட்பத்தை பயன்படுத்தி நெல் விளைச்சலை அதிகரித்துள்ளனர். இது மற்ற மாநிலங்களுக்கு முன்மாதிரி.

input_text: அரசு நிறுவனங்கள் ஆண்டுதோறும் கோடிக்கணக்கில் நஷ்டமடைகின்றன, ஆனால் உயர் அதிகாரிகள் சம்பளம் குறையவில்லை. பொறுப்புக்கூறல் என்ற வார்த்தையே இல்லை.
reason: Criticism of GLC losses and executive compensation. Targets corporate governance in government-linked companies.

input_text: மலேசியாவில் செய்தி சுதந்திரம் இன்னும் மேம்பட வேண்டும். ஊடகங்கள் அரசியல் கட்சிகளின் கட்டுப்பாட்டில் இருக்கக்கூடாது.
reason: Press freedom advocacy calling for media independence from political parties in Tamil.

reason: Reflective post about enjoying rainy season in Malaysia. Peaceful weather appreciation.

input_text: KL的LRT和MRT确实方便了很多，但最后一哩路的问题还是没解决。从车站到家还是要叫Grab，不便宜啊。
reason: Balanced feedback on public transport praising MRT but criticizing last-mile connectivity.

input_text: 刚在拉曼大学开始新学期，校园里的图书馆重新装修了，多了很多自习室。学习环境真的很不错。
reason: Post about new semester at UTAR with improved library facilities. Positive campus life content.

input_text: 网上那些说能快速赚钱的广告千万别信，我朋友就是被骗了几千块。大家小心啊。
reason: Scam awareness warning based on friend's experience. Digital safety content.

input_text: 大马的宽带网速虽然有所改善，但和邻国新加坡相比还有很大差距。电信公司应该加大对光纤网络的投资。
reason: Comparison of Malaysia's broadband speeds with Singapore, calling for more fiber network investment.

input_text: 税制改革不能再拖了。中产阶级承担了过重的税负，而真正的高收入者有各种避税手段。要公平，就从税改开始。
reason: Tax reform advocacy highlighting middle-class burden and wealth inequality in Chinese. Fiscal fairness demand.

input_text: 九皇爷诞的素食街非常热闹。各种素食小吃琳琅满目，价格也很实惠。每年这个时候都会特地去逛。
reason: Post about Nine Emperor Gods Festival vegetarian street food. Celebrates religious festival food culture.

input_text: 吉隆坡的交通真的让人崩溃，每天上班堵一两个小时。不过想想至少有车听歌，就当是移动KTV吧。
reason: Humorous take on KL traffic jams with a positive coping mechanism. Everyday commuter content.

input_text: 我每天走路去巴刹买菜，顺便当运动。新鲜蔬菜水果比超市便宜，走一圈也消耗了不少卡路里，一举两得。
reason: Combined health and frugal living tip about walking to wet market. Positive lifestyle content.

input_text: 大马的天气就是一个字：热！不过热归热，冰kacang和ABC冰一吃，什么烦恼都没了。这就是热带生活的好处。
reason: Weather complaint about Malaysian heat with dessert solution. Casual daily life post.

input_text: 数字银行牌照的发放标志着马来西亚金融科技行业的新里程碑。这将有助于提高金融普惠性，特别是对于没有银行账户的群体。
reason: Discussing Malaysia's digital banking licenses as a milestone for financial inclusion of unbanked populations.

input_text: 昨天邻居阿姨送了一大盘她自己做的娘惹糕过来，我们回送了一锅肉骨茶。这就是马来西亚邻里之间的温暖。
reason: Neighbourly food exchange story between Malaysian families. Positive community content.

input_text: 数字经济转型不是只靠喊口号就能实现的。MyDigital蓝图推出这么久了，实际进展在哪里？政府需要拿出具体成果。
reason: Digital economy policy criticism questioning MyDigital implementation progress in Chinese.

input_text: 大马的华人合作社在乡村地区做了很多贡献。从提供贷款到帮助农民销售农产品，这种互助模式很值得推广。
reason: Praising Chinese cooperative contributions in rural areas. Positive discussion of cooperative economic models.

input_text: 基础设施建设不能只看数量不看质量。新建的道路两三年就破损，排水系统年年堵塞。维护预算去了哪里？
reason: Infrastructure maintenance criticism questioning quality and maintenance spending in Chinese.

input_text: 沙巴的神山是马来西亚最高的山峰，每年吸引很多登山爱好者。我们需要确保旅游业不会破坏当地原住民的生活方式。
reason: Discussion about tourism impact on Mount Kinabalu and indigenous communities. Advocates for responsible tourism.

input_text: 槟城的交通越来越塞了，LRT说了很多年还是没有。几时才会有公共交通的改善啊？
reason: Complaint about Penang traffic and delayed public transport projects. Normal infrastructure criticism.

input_text: 马来西亚的锡器工艺享誉世界。皇家雪兰莪的锡器制作过程真的很精细，每一件都是艺术品。推荐大家去参观他们的工厂！
reason: Chinese-language appreciation of Royal Selangor pewterwork craftsmanship and factory tour.

input_text: 马来西亚的大学生活很丰富。我在厦门大学马来西亚分校参加了好几个社团，有书法社、辩论队和羽毛球队。
reason: Post about student club activities at Xiamen University Malaysia. Active campus life content.

input_text: 住在山坡上真的要注意安全，最近雨季土崩的新闻越来越多。政府应该严格管制山坡发展项目。
reason: Safety concern about hillside landslides during rainy season. Calls for stricter development regulations.

input_text: 令吉持续贬值反映了经济结构性问题。政府不能只怪外部因素，必须进行真正的经济改革，提升国家竞争力。
reason: Ringgit depreciation criticism about structural economic issues. Reform advocacy in Chinese.

input_text: 青年人参与政治不只是投票那么简单。参加社区对话、关注政策讨论、向民意代表反映问题，这些都是公民参与的方式。
reason: Encouraging diverse forms of youth political participation beyond voting. Positive civic engagement message.

input_text: 结婚酒席我们选了在餐馆办，一桌RM1200，总共20桌。比酒店便宜很多，味道还更好。推荐预算有限的新人考虑。
reason: Wedding planning advice about restaurant reception as budget option. Practical content.

input_text: 每次回怡保，一定要去那间老字号吃炸鸡翅粉。从小吃到大，味道一点都没变。这种老味道越来越难找了。
reason: Nostalgic Ipoh traditional food visit. Heritage food appreciation.

input_text: 小生意越来越难做。大商场大连锁店到处开，我们这些杂货店根本竞争不了。政府能不能给小商家一些支持？
reason: Small shop owner describing competition from large chains. Plea for government support for small businesses.

input_text: 马来西亚的榴莲出口到中国越来越多了。以前只有泰国的，现在猫山王已经成为中国市场的抢手货。对果农来说是天大的好消息。
reason: Malaysian durian export growth to China market. Positive agricultural trade news for farmers.

input_text: 马来西亚的网络安全市场正在快速成长。越来越多的本地公司提供专业的安全服务，这对保护国家数字基础设施至关重要。
reason: Positive commentary on Malaysia's growing cybersecurity industry and its importance for national digital infrastructure.

input_text: Penang food is overrated la, people go there and act like it's some culinary paradise
reason: Dismissive snarky commentary belittling Penang's food culture and those who appreciate it.
suggested_rewrite: Penang food is popular but taste is subjective. Every state in Malaysia has its own culinary strengths.

input_text: Why is everything so expensive in Malaysia nowadays? Minimum wage barely covers basic expenses. Something needs to change.
reason: Economic frustration about cost of living vs minimum wage, common populist concern.
suggested_rewrite: The gap between minimum wage and cost of living is concerning. A comprehensive review of wages, price controls, and social safety nets is needed.

input_text: No sidewalks at all in this housing area. Pedestrians have to share road with cars. Urban planning fail.
reason: Absence of sidewalks in residential area endangering pedestrians, planning failure.
suggested_rewrite: Pedestrian sidewalks are a basic safety requirement in residential areas. Request their installation through your local councilor's office.

input_text: If you're going to live in Malaysia, at least learn to speak BM la. These people want equal rights but can't even read the Rukun Negara.
reason: Linking language ability to worthiness of equal rights, which is discriminatory logic even if promoting BM is patriotic. The 'these people' framing others as outsiders.
suggested_rewrite: Proficiency in BM is important for national unity. I'd support programs that help all Malaysians improve their BM skills while respecting our multilingual heritage.

input_text: Building fire escape door always locked. If got fire how to escape? Management playing with people's lives.
reason: Fire safety violation with locked escape doors, life-threatening negligence.
suggested_rewrite: Locked fire escape doors are a serious safety violation. Report immediately to BOMBA and the local council. This is not just a complaint - it's a legal requirement.

input_text: Toll booths still using manual payment in 2024. Even warung tepi jalan accept QR payment now. Highway companies living in the stone age.
reason: Mocks highway companies as technologically backward. Dismissive comparison to roadside stalls.
suggested_rewrite: Highway payment systems could benefit from wider adoption of cashless technology to reduce congestion at toll plazas.

input_text: The playground in our taman hasn't been maintained for years. Swings broken, slide rusty. Kids can get tetanus.
reason: Unmaintained playground posing health risk to children, tetanus concern.
suggested_rewrite: Unmaintained playground equipment is a safety hazard. Report the condition with photos to the local council's infrastructure department.

input_text: These rental scams are getting worse. People ask for deposit and then the property doesn't even exist. Police don't care.
reason: Frustrated about rental scams and perceived police indifference to fraud.
suggested_rewrite: Always verify property ownership before paying deposits. Scam reports should be filed with police and CCID for investigation.

input_text: Tamil movies fans are so dramatic. Everything also cry cry cry. No wonder la their drama in real life also same level. Overreact king.
reason: Mocking Tamil cinema fans and by extension Tamil people as dramatic, linking movie preferences to ethnic character.
suggested_rewrite: Different film industries have their own styles. Tamil cinema has a rich storytelling tradition that resonates with many viewers.

input_text: Single at 35 and proud? Please la. Society has standards for a reason. Don't normalise being alone forever. It's not empowerment, it's just sad
reason: Dismisses single people's autonomy and labels their choice as 'sad'. Conflates social norms with universal standards and shames those who don't conform.
suggested_rewrite: Being single or married are both valid life choices. What matters is that people are fulfilled and happy, whatever path they choose.

input_text: Temple near my house every weekend got loud prayer until 10pm. I'm not against religion la but got consideration or not?
reason: Complaint about temple prayer noise framed with eye-rolling tone. Dismissive of religious practices despite qualifying statement.
suggested_rewrite: The temple nearby has loud prayers until late evening on weekends. A respectful conversation about noise levels could help.

input_text: Why do we even have a consumer tribunal if complaints take years to resolve? The whole system is broken.
reason: Frustrated about slow consumer tribunal process, calling the system broken.
suggested_rewrite: The consumer tribunal process could be expedited with more resources and digital case management. Timely resolution builds consumer confidence.

input_text: These Gen Z workers want work-life balance but don't even produce quality work. Entitled la
reason: Condescending generalization dismissing Gen Z workers as entitled and unproductive.
suggested_rewrite: Gen Z workers value work-life balance. Organisations could find ways to align this with productivity goals.

input_text: These interns nowadays expect everything to be spoon-fed, no initiative at all
reason: Condescending generalization about all current interns as lacking initiative.
suggested_rewrite: Some interns may need more guidance. A structured mentoring approach can help develop their initiative.

input_text: Public swimming pool schedule so limited. Only open 3 hours on weekdays. Working people can never use it.
reason: Frustrated about limited public pool hours excluding working people.
suggested_rewrite: Public swimming pool hours should accommodate working schedules. Extended evening hours would make facilities more accessible to all residents.

input_text: Why are we still building new mosques when some areas don't even have proper schools? Priorities, people.
reason: Pitting religious infrastructure against education in a way that could be read as dismissing the importance of mosques. Resource allocation critique with a religiously insensitive undertone.
suggested_rewrite: I think we should ensure balanced development that includes both educational facilities and places of worship. Both serve important community needs.

input_text: That YB's son got off with just a fine for drunk driving. Rich Malay privilege at its finest.
reason: Racializing a class/privilege issue by attributing judicial leniency to 'Rich Malay privilege,' which conflates legitimate frustration about double standards with ethnic targeting.
suggested_rewrite: It's concerning when politically connected individuals seem to receive lighter sentences. The justice system should apply equally regardless of status.

input_text: Hawker food portions getting smaller but price keeps going up. These sellers think we don't notice.
reason: Frustrated about shrinkflation at hawker stalls, consumer complaint.
suggested_rewrite: Rising ingredient costs may affect portions. If you feel pricing is unfair, share constructive feedback with the vendor or choose alternatives.

input_text: I saw the new councillor's salary slip being shared on Twitter. RM15k for doing nothing. These bloodsuckers.
reason: Endorses sharing a private salary slip online and insults the councillor as a bloodsucker.
suggested_rewrite: Councillor remuneration should be publicly disclosed through official channels. Sharing private documents without consent raises privacy concerns.

input_text: Local university graduates cannot even write proper English email. Then want to compete with Singapore graduates. Dream on la.
reason: Dismissive insult to all local graduates, unfavourable comparison with Singapore. Demoralising and overgeneralised.
suggested_rewrite: English proficiency among some local graduates could be improved. Universities should focus on strengthening communication skills in their curriculum.

input_text: Satire: 'Our road quality is world-class. Just ask the potholes.' Government excellence at work, folks.
reason: Sarcastic mockery of government infrastructure with contemptuous tone. The point about road quality is valid but delivered with derision that targets governance broadly.
suggested_rewrite: Road conditions in many areas need serious attention. I hope the local authorities prioritize infrastructure maintenance for public safety.

input_text: Multi-storey car park built by government already crumbling. Concrete falling on cars. Building was just completed 3 years ago.
reason: New government car park structurally failing within 3 years, construction quality scandal.
suggested_rewrite: Structural failure of a 3-year-old building warrants investigation. Report to JKR and the local council. Engineering assessments should determine if it's safe to use.

input_text: I think Grab drivers purposely take long route so the fare go up. All same tactic one. Don't think passengers are stupid la bro.
reason: Accusing all Grab drivers of cheating passengers, unsubstantiated blanket accusation against an entire group.
suggested_rewrite: Sometimes ride-hailing routes seem longer than expected. Passengers can report route discrepancies through the app for review.

input_text: I heard the MB got his position through connections, not merit. His degree also bought one apparently.
reason: Unverified gossip about a Chief Minister's qualifications and appointment. Spreads potentially defamatory claims as fact.
suggested_rewrite: Questions about political appointments and qualifications should be verified through official records. Spreading unconfirmed rumours can be harmful and legally risky.

input_text: Why are there so many Korean and Japanese expats opening cafes in KL? Local businesses can't even compete with their 'aesthetic' pricing. RM25 for a coffee? Go back and sell there la
reason: Targets Korean and Japanese entrepreneurs with a 'go back' sentiment. While concerns about competitive pricing are valid, the xenophobic dismissal undermines the critique.
suggested_rewrite: Premium cafes in KL are pricing coffee quite high. It would be great to see more support for local cafe owners who offer quality products at accessible prices.

input_text: Government always talk about digital transformation but their websites still crash every time there's a new registration open.
reason: Government website reliability criticism during high-demand periods.
suggested_rewrite: Government digital platforms should be built to handle peak traffic. Cloud-based scaling solutions can prevent crashes during high-demand registration periods.

input_text: Parking enforcement in this city is a complete joke. Summon the wrong people while actual offenders roam free. Bunch of clowns.
reason: Calls parking enforcers clowns, going beyond critique of inconsistency into name-calling.
suggested_rewrite: Inconsistent parking enforcement undermines public trust. A more systematic and fair approach to issuing summons would address legitimate complaints.

input_text: Every time there's a flood, same story. Government promises, opposition blames, and we the rakyat suffer. Useless lot, all of them.
reason: Emotionally charged blanket dismissal of all politicians as 'useless' during a crisis. While expressing legitimate frustration, the sweeping contempt undermines constructive discourse.
suggested_rewrite: Flood response seems to follow the same pattern every year — promises and blame. I wish both government and opposition would prioritize actionable disaster preparedness instead.

input_text: Insurance agent friend keep pushing me to buy policy. So annoying la, friendship also become business. Like parasite only.
reason: Compares friend who sells insurance to a parasite. Rude dehumanizing language about someone's profession.
suggested_rewrite: My friend who sells insurance keeps approaching me about policies. I wish they'd respect boundaries between friendship and business.

input_text: Home internet installation appointment keeps getting pushed back. Already waited 3 weeks. How do I work from home without internet?
reason: Repeated internet installation delays affecting work-from-home capability.
suggested_rewrite: Escalate delayed installations to the ISP's complaints department and MCMC. Consider a portable hotspot as a temporary solution while waiting.

input_text: Every time election comes, politicians promise the sky. After winning, disappear. Malaysian politics is a joke.
reason: Cynical dismissal of Malaysian politics as a joke, disillusionment with electoral promises.
suggested_rewrite: Accountability for election promises should be tracked systematically. Voters can hold representatives accountable through town halls and public feedback sessions.

input_text: You're 34 and still renting? That's embarrassing la. Most people your age already have their own house.
reason: Shames someone for renting at 34, ignoring the reality of high property prices in Malaysia.
suggested_rewrite: Many Malaysians rent due to high property prices. Home ownership timelines vary based on individual financial circumstances.

input_text: Government hospital only gives generic medicine. Branded one have to buy yourself. Two-tier healthcare system.
reason: Generic vs branded medicine complaint at government hospitals, two-tier system critique.
suggested_rewrite: Generic medicines are equally effective and clinically tested. If you prefer specific brands, discuss options with your doctor.

input_text: This hospital makes you pay deposit before treating emergency cases. Isn't that against medical ethics?
reason: Hospital requiring deposit for emergency treatment, ethical and legal concern.
suggested_rewrite: Emergency cases should not be delayed for payment. If a hospital demands deposits before treating emergencies, report to the Ministry of Health.

input_text: My elderly mother fell at the wet market because there's no proper drainage. Floor always slippery. Nobody accountable.
reason: Elderly person falling at wet market due to slippery undrained floors, safety hazard.
suggested_rewrite: Wet markets need proper drainage and anti-slip flooring. Document the incident and report to the market management and local council for safety improvements.

input_text: Selangor people think they're so sophisticated just because they live near KL. Come to Perak and see real culture, not your overpriced hipster cafes.
reason: Passive-aggressive inter-state comparison that dismisses Selangor lifestyle as superficial and positions Perak as culturally superior.
suggested_rewrite: Both Selangor and Perak have unique cultural offerings. Malaysia's diversity means every state has something special to appreciate.

input_text: The new MRT so expensive to ride daily. Monthly pass cost almost same as parking. Where's the incentive to use public transport?
reason: MRT pricing not competitive enough to incentivize public transport use.
suggested_rewrite: Public transport pricing should be competitive with driving costs to encourage ridership. Subsidized monthly passes could make MRT more attractive for daily commuters.

input_text: This mamak restaurant double charges by adding service tax that doesn't appear on the receipt. Sneaky accounting.
reason: Restaurant adding hidden charges not on receipt, tax fraud suspicion.
suggested_rewrite: Service charges must be clearly stated on receipts. Report undisclosed charges to KPDNHEP and request detailed billing before paying.

input_text: Gig workers blocking restaurant entrances with their motorbikes waiting for orders. Zero consideration for actual customers.
reason: Dismisses delivery riders as inconsiderate while elevating 'actual customers' above them. Creates an us-vs-them dynamic.
suggested_rewrite: Designated waiting areas for delivery riders near restaurants would improve the experience for everyone, including riders and walk-in customers.

input_text: Malaysia's education system produces robots, not thinkers. No wonder we keep losing talent to other countries.
reason: Harsh criticism of education system with brain drain concern, disparaging tone.
suggested_rewrite: Malaysia's education system could benefit from more emphasis on critical thinking and creativity to retain homegrown talent.

input_text: Every time government launches new system sure got problem. MyKad, MySejahtera, now this also down. When will they learn?
reason: Frustrated pattern-observation about government IT system failures, sarcastic but constructive core concern.
suggested_rewrite: Government digital systems have a pattern of launch-day issues. Investing in better testing and infrastructure before launch would save time and public frustration.

input_text: You know what's funny? The same people who say 'Malaysia for all' are the ones who won't rent to non-Bumis. Hypocrites.
reason: Calling out rental discrimination but doing so by labeling an entire group as hypocrites, which is itself a generalization that inflames rather than addresses the issue.
suggested_rewrite: Rental discrimination based on ethnicity is a real issue that needs to be addressed. I hope we can push for anti-discrimination policies in housing.

input_text: Clinic appointment at 10am but doctor only came at 12. No apology, no explanation. We're just numbers to them.
reason: Doctor lateness without apology, feeling dehumanized by healthcare system.
suggested_rewrite: Patient wait times and doctor punctuality should be tracked and improved. Formal feedback to the clinic can help drive accountability.

input_text: EPF withdrawal for housing deposit gone in 2 months of processing. By the time money comes, property already sold to someone else.
reason: EPF withdrawal processing too slow for time-sensitive property purchases.
suggested_rewrite: EPF should expedite housing withdrawal processing. Consider pre-approval facilities so funds are ready when needed for property purchases.

input_text: Fresh graduate expected to have 5 years experience for entry level job. How does that even make sense?
reason: Absurd job requirement frustration for entry-level positions demanding extensive experience.
suggested_rewrite: Unrealistic job requirements discourage fresh graduates. Employers should consider internship programs and on-the-job training for entry-level positions.

input_text: Those Bangla workers in the condo, confirm they pack 20 people in one unit. No wonder the lift always rosak.
reason: Xenophobic stereotype about Bangladeshi workers overcrowding condos. Uses derogatory shorthand 'Bangla' and blames them for infrastructure issues.
suggested_rewrite: Overcrowding in condos is a management issue. The JMB should enforce occupancy limits equally for all tenants.

input_text: Just saw this on IG: 'Chinese Malaysians only care about money, not about the country.' Comment section is wild.
reason: Sharing a racist stereotype about Chinese Malaysians from Instagram. Noting the comment section is 'wild' draws attention without condemning, effectively amplifying the racist content.
suggested_rewrite: I saw a post with harmful stereotypes about Chinese Malaysians. Every community contributes to nation-building in different ways. Let's not amplify divisive content.

input_text: Pharmacy sell medicine close to expiry at full price. Should give discount at least. Unfair to consumers.
reason: Near-expiry medicines sold at full price, consumer fairness complaint.
suggested_rewrite: Check expiry dates before purchasing medicines. If near-expiry items are sold at full price, raise it with the pharmacy or report to NPRA.

input_text: Overheard someone say the water treatment plant in PJ is contaminated but being covered up. Scary if true.
reason: Amplifies an unverified secondhand claim about water contamination that could cause public panic.
suggested_rewrite: Water quality concerns should be directed to SPAN or the local water authority for verification. Official testing results are publicly available on their website.

input_text: The public park is so dirty and unkept. Broken equipment everywhere. Kids can get hurt. Nobody maintains anything in this country.
reason: Frustrated park maintenance complaint with sweeping country-level generalization.
suggested_rewrite: The park's broken equipment poses safety risks for children. I'll report the specific hazards to the local council for urgent maintenance.

input_text: Grab riders speeding through my taman like it's a race track. One day someone will get hurt and then only they'll learn. Morons.
reason: Calls riders morons and wishes for an accident as a learning moment, rude and insensitive.
suggested_rewrite: Speeding through residential areas is a serious safety concern. Speed bumps and community awareness campaigns could help prevent accidents before they happen.

input_text: Clinic queue from 6am but doctor only arrives at 9. Then break at 12. What kind of healthcare is this?
reason: Frustrated about healthcare queuing vs doctor availability, sarcastic tone.
suggested_rewrite: Healthcare appointment systems could reduce wait times by scheduling patients in time slots rather than requiring early morning queueing.

input_text: Why do influencers always have to show off their sponsored trips? Nobody cares about your free hotel stay. Get a real job.
reason: Dismisses influencing as not a real job and rudely tells them nobody cares, personal and snarky.
suggested_rewrite: Content creation is a legitimate career, though excessive sponsored content can feel inauthentic. A balance between sponsored and genuine content would be more engaging for audiences.

input_text: Chinese aunties at the market bargain until the seller lose money also they don't care. So kiasu one, everything also want cheapest. No empathy.
reason: Ethnic stereotype of Chinese aunties as kiasu and lacking empathy, negative generalisation based on ethnicity.
suggested_rewrite: Aggressive bargaining at markets can be frustrating for sellers. Fair pricing benefits both buyers and sellers.

input_text: Proton car quality still not great but price keeps increasing. Local brand should be affordable and reliable right?
reason: National car brand quality vs price criticism, affordability expectation.
suggested_rewrite: National car brands should balance quality improvements with competitive pricing. Consumer feedback through official channels helps drive product development.

input_text: Food delivery rider nearly hit my kid in the condo. These riders think they own the road, speed like crazy in residential area.
reason: Generalizes all delivery riders as reckless based on one incident. Emotionally charged but overgeneralizing.
suggested_rewrite: A delivery rider was speeding in our condo area and nearly hit my child. Speed limits in residential areas need better enforcement.

input_text: Apartment corridor lights always off. Walking to my unit in total darkness every night. Falling hazard for elderly.
reason: Dark apartment corridors creating falling hazard especially for elderly residents.
suggested_rewrite: Corridor lighting is a basic safety requirement. Report to building management and if unresolved, escalate to the Commissioner of Buildings.

input_text: Chow Kit area is just hopeless la. Dirty, smelly, full of dodgy people. Don't know why government don't just clean up the whole place.
reason: Snide remarks about Chow Kit neighborhood and its residents, calling them 'dodgy people'. Area-based prejudice.
suggested_rewrite: Chow Kit faces cleanliness and safety challenges. Urban renewal and community support could greatly improve the area.

input_text: Parking lot never got covered spaces but rains every day. Come back to car drenched inside because window seal broken from sun damage.
reason: Uncovered parking lots causing vehicle damage from weather exposure.
suggested_rewrite: Covered parking should be a standard feature in tropical climate buildings. Raise this at the building management meeting.

input_text: Parking attendant at the mall acted like he owns the place. So rude, pointing and shouting like we are his servant.
reason: Rude complaint about parking attendant's behavior. Exaggerated and emotionally charged service complaint.
suggested_rewrite: The parking attendant at the mall was quite aggressive in directing cars. A more polite approach would be appreciated.

input_text: Airport taxi queue always so long but they only allow their own cabs. Why block ride-hailing? Cartel behavior.
reason: Airport taxi monopoly blocking ride-hailing services, anti-competition complaint.
suggested_rewrite: Airport transportation should offer multiple options including ride-hailing for passenger convenience. Anti-competitive practices can be reported to MyCC.

input_text: Social media influencers really are the worst. Promoting products they've never used. Zero integrity, all about money.
reason: Cynical generalization about influencer ethics, frustrated consumer trust issue.
suggested_rewrite: Consumers should be aware that influencer endorsements may not always reflect genuine experiences. Better advertising disclosure rules would help build trust.

input_text: The government housing loan interest rate higher than commercial banks. Isn't government supposed to help the rakyat?
reason: Government housing loan interest higher than commercial, contradicting public welfare mandate.
suggested_rewrite: Compare all available housing loan options including government and commercial banks. Treasury Housing Loan rates are reviewed periodically.

input_text: The drainage system in this new housing estate is terrible. After rain, water takes 3 days to subside. Design fault clearly.
reason: New housing estate with poor drainage taking 3 days to clear after rain.
suggested_rewrite: Poor drainage in new estates may violate building standards. Document the issue and raise it with the developer and local council's engineering department.

input_text: My Malay housemate never cleans the common area. Always leave dishes in the sink for days. These people same la, at home got maid do everything for them.
reason: Attributing messiness to ethnicity with 'these people', implying it's a Malay trait. Ethnic generalisation.
suggested_rewrite: My housemate doesn't clean the common area regularly. We need to have a house meeting to set cleaning expectations.

input_text: Insurance company always find excuse not to pay claims. You pay premiums for years and when you need it, suddenly not covered.
reason: Frustrated about insurance claim rejections after paying premiums, consumer distrust.
suggested_rewrite: Insurance claim disputes can be escalated to Bank Negara's BNMTELELINK or the Financial Ombudsman. Keep all documentation for your claim.

input_text: The staff at HKL emergency department have the warmth of a frozen fish. You could be bleeding out and they'd still ask you to take a number.
reason: Dehumanises HKL emergency staff as cold and uncaring. Exaggerated attack on healthcare worker empathy.
suggested_rewrite: The emergency department at HKL can feel impersonal due to high patient volumes. Better triage communication could improve the patient experience.

input_text: Every time go government office sure got one aunty there who act like she doing YOU a favour. Excuse me, it's YOUR JOB la dey.
reason: Rude personal insult directed at civil servants with dismissive tone, though expressing legitimate frustration.
suggested_rewrite: Service at government offices can be slow and the experience frustrating. I wish the process was more efficient.

input_text: Lol this politician really said 'rakyat should be grateful.' Grateful for what? Your incompetence? What a joke.
reason: Mocking a politician's specific quote but escalating into personal insult ('your incompetence'). Crosses from quoting-and-critiquing into contemptuous attack.
suggested_rewrite: A politician recently said the public should be grateful. I think that's tone-deaf given the current challenges people face. Leaders should earn gratitude through results.

input_text: These foreign students ar, always so loud in the library. No manners one. Go back to your country and be loud there la.
reason: Mild xenophobic complaint generalising all foreign students as ill-mannered, with a dismissive 'go back' sentiment.
suggested_rewrite: It would be great if everyone in the library could keep the noise down. Maybe the university should enforce quiet zones more strictly.

input_text: Every time someone forward these 'sugar price naik 50%' messages I just laugh la. These people never learn to check facts one.
reason: Dismissive attitude toward people who share unverified price increase claims. Condescending tone while addressing misinformation.
suggested_rewrite: Unverified messages about sugar price hikes keep circulating. It's important to check official sources before sharing.

input_text: The accident victim also got fault la, who ask you to jaywalk at night wearing dark clothes. Common sense where?
reason: Victim-blaming in accident context. Dismissive of victim's suffering with condescending 'common sense' remark.
suggested_rewrite: Pedestrian safety is important. Wearing visible clothing at night and using crossings can help prevent accidents.

input_text: Malaysian school discipline teachers are basically dictators with a cane. No wonder kids grow up scared of authority.
reason: Labels discipline teachers as dictators. Inflammatory comparison that demeans educators while raising valid corporal punishment concerns.
suggested_rewrite: School discipline methods should prioritize positive reinforcement. Moving away from punitive approaches can create a healthier learning environment.

input_text: Public toilet at the bus station charges 30 sen but there's no soap, no tissue, and the flush doesn't work. What are we paying for?
reason: Paying for substandard public toilet facilities, consumer rights complaint.
suggested_rewrite: Paid public toilets should meet basic hygiene standards. Report substandard facilities to the local council or the toilet operator's management.

input_text: Weekend farmers market only has organic produce at premium prices. Regular farmers with affordable produce not given space.
reason: Farmers market excluding affordable produce in favor of premium organic only.
suggested_rewrite: Farmers markets should accommodate a range of price points to serve all income levels. Dedicated sections for affordable local produce would increase accessibility.

input_text: Why do B40 people keep having children they can't afford? Then they complain about not having enough money. It's basic logic.
reason: Judges B40 reproductive choices and implies they lack basic logic, a classist and dismissive comment.
suggested_rewrite: Family planning support and resources should be accessible to all income groups. Financial challenges are complex and require systemic solutions.

input_text: Toll rate increase again but road quality still the same. Potholes everywhere. Where does the money go?
reason: Toll rate increasing while road quality remains poor with potholes.
suggested_rewrite: Toll concessionaires are required to maintain road quality. Report potholes through PLUS or highway operator apps and file complaints with LLM if standards aren't met.

input_text: Chinese tourists are the worst la. So loud, spit everywhere, no respect for our country.
reason: Xenophobic generalisation about Chinese tourists using negative stereotypes. Brands an entire nationality with the worst behaviours of a few.
suggested_rewrite: Some tourists can be inconsiderate regardless of nationality. Better signage and cultural guides at popular spots could help.

input_text: Church group every Sunday block the whole road for parking. Other people want to use road also cannot. So inconsiderate.
reason: Targets church-goers specifically as inconsiderate for parking issues. Religious dimension adds sensitivity.
suggested_rewrite: Sunday parking near the church causes road congestion. Better parking arrangements would benefit the whole community.

input_text: Why do some people still litter everywhere? So uncivilized. Like living in a kampung mentality.
reason: Calling litterers 'uncivilized' with classist 'kampung mentality' remark, mildly insulting but behavior-directed.
suggested_rewrite: Littering remains a problem that affects our communities. More public bins and awareness campaigns could help encourage better habits.

input_text: Haiya these Mainland tourists come here buy everything, make everything expensive for us locals.
reason: Mild xenophobic complaint blaming tourists from China for price increases. Oversimplifies economic dynamics into an us-vs-them narrative.
suggested_rewrite: Tourism contributes to the economy, but we need policies to ensure local residents aren't priced out. It's a policy issue, not a blame game.

input_text: Parking structure at the mall is confusing. No clear signs. Spent 20 minutes trying to find my car. Stressful with kids.
reason: Mall parking structure has poor signage causing stress for families.
