suggested_rewrite: Mall parking should have clear zone markers, color coding, and digital displays showing available spots. Consider using parking apps that save your location.

input_text: Customer service in Malaysia is a joke la, these people clearly don't care about their jobs
reason: Blanket insult to Malaysian customer service workers as uncaring. Rude generalization.
suggested_rewrite: Customer service standards in Malaysia could be improved. Better training and support for staff would help.

input_text: DBKL's idea of urban planning is basically slapping a new mall next to another mall. Zero creativity, maximum traffic chaos.
reason: Mocks DBKL planners as uncreative. Dismissive of urban development decisions with exaggerated criticism.
suggested_rewrite: KL's urban planning could benefit from more diverse commercial developments rather than concentrating malls in already congested areas.

input_text: This area has no pedestrian crossing for 2km. People have to cross a busy road with no traffic light. Accident waiting to happen.
reason: No pedestrian crossing for 2km forcing dangerous road crossings.
suggested_rewrite: A 2km stretch without a pedestrian crossing is a safety hazard. Request the local council to install a zebra crossing or traffic light at the busiest point.

input_text: Taxi from KLIA always overcharge tourists. Give Malaysia bad name. Authorities don't care as usual.
reason: Tourism concern about airport taxi overcharging, frustrated about enforcement and national image.
suggested_rewrite: Airport taxi overcharging affects Malaysia's tourism reputation. Stricter meter enforcement and more ride-hailing access at KLIA would help address this issue.

input_text: The new highway interchange design is so confusing. GPS also cannot navigate properly. Multiple accidents already.
reason: Confusing highway interchange causing accidents, design flaw complaint.
suggested_rewrite: Highway interchange design should be tested with user feedback before completion. Report confusing sections to the highway operator for better signage.

input_text: This developer really cut corners on the construction. Walls cracking, tiles falling, plumbing leaking. Should be ashamed.
reason: Harsh developer criticism about poor construction quality, frustrated home buyer.
suggested_rewrite: The construction quality of this development has serious defects. I'm documenting all issues for a formal complaint under the defect liability period.

input_text: My aunty forward me message saying cooking oil will be RM30 next month. I told her fake news la but she insist 'someone in government told her'. These people ah cannot be helped.
reason: Describes forwarding of unverified cooking oil price claims with condescending dismissal of people who believe them.
suggested_rewrite: Unverified claims about cooking oil prices are circulating. Encourage family members to verify with official sources like KPDNHEP.

input_text: People who double park deserve to have their cars towed and crushed. So selfish and inconsiderate!
reason: Hyperbolic angry reaction to double parking, expressing frustration with disproportionate suggested punishment.
suggested_rewrite: Double parking is a real problem that causes inconvenience for everyone. Better enforcement and parking solutions would help address this issue.

input_text: These part-time workers nowadays no work ethic one. Come late, leave early, phone non-stop. Then cry when no permanent offer.
reason: Dismissive generalization about part-time workers. Condescending and judgmental about young workers' habits.
suggested_rewrite: Some part-time workers could improve their punctuality and focus. Clear expectations from employers would help.

input_text: DBKL enforcement officers are a joke la. Tegur street vendor kecik je tapi big company langgar peraturan tak buat apa. Selective enforcement memang.
reason: Accuses DBKL of selective enforcement with a dismissive comparison but provides no specific evidence.
suggested_rewrite: Enforcement should be applied consistently regardless of business size. Citizens can report cases of non-compliance through DBKL's official channels.

input_text: Don't let a non-Indian make your banana leaf rice. The thosai will taste wrong and the sambar won't have soul.
reason: Restricts banana leaf rice preparation to only Indian cooks. Ethnic gatekeeping that dismisses culinary skill regardless of background.
suggested_rewrite: Authentic banana leaf rice requires mastery of specific spices and techniques. Skilled cooks of any background who learn properly can make excellent dishes.

input_text: Malaysia's bureaucracy makes me want to scream. Everything needs 10 forms, 5 stamps, and three months of waiting. Absolutely brain-dead system.
reason: Labels the entire bureaucratic system as brain-dead, using offensive language for a legitimate frustration.
suggested_rewrite: Reducing paperwork and streamlining approval processes would greatly improve government service delivery. Digital solutions could cut waiting times significantly.

input_text: My child's teacher gives too much homework. Kids come home from school and do homework until midnight. Where's the childhood?
reason: Excessive homework concern affecting children's wellbeing and childhood.
suggested_rewrite: Homework volume should be balanced with children's need for rest and play. Raise the concern at the parent-teacher meeting for a class-wide discussion.

input_text: Why do people still study literature or philosophy? Those degrees are completely useless in Malaysia's job market.
reason: Dismisses humanities education as useless, ignoring the diverse career paths these degrees enable.
suggested_rewrite: Literature and philosophy degrees develop critical thinking skills valued across many industries. The job market is broader than it appears for humanities graduates.

input_text: Unifi is honestly the worst ISP in Southeast Asia. Even Cambodia has better internet. What a joke of a company.
reason: Makes sweeping negative comparisons to belittle Malaysian internet infrastructure without constructive feedback.
suggested_rewrite: Internet speeds in Malaysia could be improved compared to regional benchmarks. Constructive feedback to MCMC and TM can help drive change.

input_text: My neighbour is 40 and just got engaged. The whole taman is gossiping. At that age, just stay single la, less drama.
reason: Participates in age-based gossip and advises someone to give up on marriage at 40.
suggested_rewrite: Getting engaged at 40 is a joyful milestone. It's wonderful that people find love at any stage of life, and they deserve congratulations.

input_text: My grandmother still believes putting lime on the forehead cures headaches. Old people really live in the Stone Age.
reason: Mocks traditional remedies and dismisses grandmother's generation as primitive. Ageist and culturally dismissive.
suggested_rewrite: Traditional remedies like using lime are part of our cultural heritage. While modern medicine is effective, some traditional practices can offer comfort alongside medical treatment.

input_text: This school ah, every week got different program, homework also not much but fees keep going up. What are they teaching?
reason: Dismissive sarcastic complaint about school activities and fees. Questions teaching quality rudely.
suggested_rewrite: My child's school has many programs but academic homework seems insufficient. I'd like to understand how fees are being utilised.

input_text: Women drivers la, always signal left then turn right. No spatial awareness at all. That's why insurance for women should be higher.
reason: Sexist generalisation about women drivers, suggesting discriminatory insurance pricing based on gender.
suggested_rewrite: Some drivers make confusing signals on the road. Driving skills vary among individuals regardless of gender.

input_text: Neighbor's dog bark whole night nobody say anything. But when Muslim want to azan, everyone complain noise. Hypocrites everywhere in this country.
reason: Pitting noise complaints against each other along religious lines, calling people hypocrites. Provocative framing.
suggested_rewrite: Noise complaints should be handled consistently regardless of the source. Community dialogue can help find solutions that respect everyone.

input_text: KWSP investment returns getting worse every year but management still getting bonuses. Classic Malaysian institution mentality — fail upwards.
reason: Cynical criticism of KWSP management bonuses despite declining returns. Generalises to all Malaysian institutions.
suggested_rewrite: KWSP management compensation should be tied to investment performance. Transparency in bonus structures would build contributor confidence.

input_text: My EPF savings not enough for retirement. Government keep letting us withdraw. Who protects our old age?
reason: EPF withdrawal policy criticism with retirement adequacy concern.
suggested_rewrite: Balancing EPF withdrawals with retirement adequacy is important. Consider supplementary retirement savings and review EPF's long-term financial planning tools.

input_text: Local council planted trees but never water or maintain them. All dead within months. Wasted taxpayer money.
reason: Council-planted trees dying from lack of maintenance, wasteful spending.
suggested_rewrite: Tree planting programs should include maintenance schedules. Report dead or dying municipal trees to the landscaping department for replacement and proper care.

input_text: Property prices in Penang are insane because of foreign buyers snapping everything up. Regular Penangites can't even afford a flat. Thanks to our government for selling us out
reason: Blames foreign buyers for Penang property prices and accuses the government of betrayal. While foreign investment impacts are debatable, the 'selling us out' rhetoric is inflammatory.
suggested_rewrite: Property prices in Penang have risen significantly, partly due to foreign investment. Policies like higher foreign buyer thresholds could help ensure local buyers aren't priced out.

input_text: Every time election comes the politicians start giving out money. So obvious la the bribery
reason: Cynical accusation of widespread election bribery without nuance. Dismissive take on political processes.
suggested_rewrite: Monetary handouts during election season raise concerns about ethical campaign practices and should be scrutinized.

input_text: Those Chinese aunties at the pasar always so kiasu, push and shove to get the best produce. No manners at all.
reason: Racial and ageist stereotype about Chinese aunties being kiasu (afraid to lose). Singles out a group by ethnicity and age.
suggested_rewrite: Wet markets can get crowded and competitive. Better queue management and stall organisation would make shopping more pleasant for everyone.

input_text: Motorcycle gang in our taman every night so noisy. These low-class people ruining the neighbourhood.
reason: Labels motorcyclists as 'low-class' ruining the neighbourhood. Classist language dehumanises them.
suggested_rewrite: Nighttime noise from motorcycles is a legitimate concern. Residents can report this to local authorities for better enforcement of noise regulations.

input_text: This new colleague always speak Mandarin in meetings even though got Malay and Indian people. So rude la. They think only Chinese matter is it?
reason: While the concern about inclusivity is valid, 'they think only Chinese matter' escalates to an ethnic accusation.
suggested_rewrite: Using a common language in mixed meetings is important for inclusivity. Perhaps a gentle reminder about the meeting language policy would help.

input_text: These people who take KTM every day, I really pity them la, must be tough being poor
reason: Condescending elitist pity toward KTM commuters, equating public transport use with poverty.
suggested_rewrite: KTM commuters face challenges like delays. Public transport infrastructure needs more investment.

input_text: My colleague got promoted just because her uncle is the GM. Merit means nothing here
reason: Accusation of nepotism targeting a specific colleague. Rude and dismissive of the individual's possible merits.
suggested_rewrite: I feel the promotion process may lack transparency. A merit-based system would be fairer for all employees.

input_text: School prefects in Malaysia are just power-tripping kids with a badge. They bully younger students and teachers look the other way.
reason: Labels all school prefects as bullies and accuses teachers of complicity. Sweeping accusation against school authority structures.
suggested_rewrite: Some students may misuse prefect authority. Schools should have clear accountability systems and channels for reporting concerns.

input_text: Government hospital appointment system is hopeless. Given date 4 months away for specialist. Might as well self-medicate.
reason: 4-month wait for specialist appointment at government hospital.
suggested_rewrite: Long specialist waits can be managed by requesting urgent referrals from your GP. Some hospitals offer walk-in specialist clinics for pressing cases.

input_text: My kid's school toilet is so dirty they refuse to use it. Come home every day holding it in. Health issue right there.
reason: School toilet so dirty children avoid using it, causing health issues.
suggested_rewrite: Clean school toilets are a health necessity. Raise this at the PIBG meeting and report to PPD if the school doesn't address it.

input_text: Graduate unemployment rate so high but government keep saying economy is growing. Growing for who exactly?
reason: Questioning who benefits from economic growth amid high graduate unemployment.
suggested_rewrite: Aligning economic growth with graduate employment requires industry-academia collaboration and skills development programs targeted at high-demand sectors.

input_text: Online shopping in Malaysia really cannot make it. Half the time package comes damaged. Quality control where?
reason: Frustrated e-commerce complaint about damaged packages, common consumer frustration expressed bluntly.
suggested_rewrite: Package handling in Malaysian e-commerce needs improvement. Better packaging standards and accountability for logistics companies would help reduce damaged deliveries.

input_text: These Dato types always park their Cayenne in handicapped spots. Rules don't apply when you have a title apparently.
reason: Stereotypes all titled individuals as entitled rule-breakers. Sweeping generalization that fuels class resentment.
suggested_rewrite: Some individuals park in handicapped spots regardless of rules. Enforcement should apply equally to everyone, regardless of status.

input_text: Every time got flood, same story. Government just gives out nasi bungkus and takes photos. After that, gone. Total joke.
reason: Cynically reduces disaster relief efforts to photo opportunities with a dismissive and mocking tone.
suggested_rewrite: Disaster response needs to go beyond immediate relief. Long-term flood mitigation plans and consistent follow-up support for affected communities are essential.

input_text: Penang bridge toll for what? Every raya jam 6 hours, no improvement in decades. Might as well swim across la cheaper.
reason: Sarcastic dismissal of Penang bridge toll value. Exaggerated rhetoric about congestion with no acknowledgment of infrastructure efforts.
suggested_rewrite: Penang bridge congestion during festive seasons remains a challenge. Alternative solutions like improved public transport connections could complement the existing infrastructure.

input_text: The food court hygiene is terrible. I saw a rat running across the floor. These operators don't care about customers at all.
reason: Harsh food court hygiene complaint with sweeping generalization about operators.
suggested_rewrite: I witnessed a hygiene issue at the food court that should be reported to the local health department for inspection.

input_text: That new policy is so stupid. Whoever came up with it must have been sleeping during meetings.
reason: Sarcastic dismissal of a policy and implied incompetence of its creators, rude but not dangerous.
suggested_rewrite: I have concerns about the new policy. It seems like some aspects weren't fully thought through. Here are some specific issues that need addressing.

input_text: Married couples who post 'happily married' content every day on social media are usually the ones with the most problems. Just saying. Don't be fooled
reason: Makes a cynical generalisation about married couples' social media behaviour, implying those who share positivity are hiding problems. This is judgmental and could be hurtful.
suggested_rewrite: Social media only shows one side of people's lives. It's best not to compare or make assumptions about others' relationships based on what they post online.

input_text: That MP ah, every time open mouth confirm say something stupid one. How this kind of people become leader?
reason: Mocks an MP's intelligence repeatedly. Dismissive personal attack on a public figure's competence.
suggested_rewrite: That MP's public statements often lack substance. Voters deserve better-informed representation.

input_text: Malaysian schools care more about the colour of your socks than actual education quality. Priorities right?
reason: Sarcastically dismisses Malaysian schools as focusing on trivial rules over education. Undermines public confidence in education system.
suggested_rewrite: While uniform standards have their place, it's important that schools also prioritize academic quality and student wellbeing.

input_text: Saw someone post the IC number of that rude grab driver online. Honestly he deserves it for being so rude to passengers.
reason: Endorses doxxing a person's IC number as justified punishment for rudeness, normalizing privacy violations.
suggested_rewrite: Sharing someone's personal identification details online is a serious privacy violation. If a driver was rude, the proper channel is to report through the Grab app.

input_text: I'm not against foreign workers but my neighbourhood is starting to feel unsafe since so many moved in. Lots of noise at night and rubbish everywhere. Just stating facts
reason: Uses the 'just stating facts' disclaimer to link foreign workers moving in with safety and cleanliness concerns. Associates foreigners with neighbourhood decline.
suggested_rewrite: My neighbourhood has had noise and litter issues recently. Would be good to work with the local council and residents' association to address these concerns for everyone.

input_text: Tan Sri retiring from GLC and immediately becoming chairman of another GLC. Musical chairs for the elite while we watch.
reason: Sarcastically criticizes revolving door appointments among titled elites. While raising valid governance concern, tone is dismissive and inflammatory.
suggested_rewrite: The practice of rotating leadership among GLCs raises governance questions. Transparent appointment processes with fresh talent would strengthen public trust.

input_text: Every developer in KL builds luxury condos no one can afford. Meanwhile young people like us share rooms like sardines. But sure, keep building for foreign investors
reason: Valid housing concern but the sarcastic tone and blame on foreign investors oversimplifies the issue. Creates a resentful narrative without acknowledging complex market dynamics.
suggested_rewrite: There's an oversupply of luxury condos in KL while affordable housing remains scarce. Developers and policymakers should prioritise housing that meets the needs of young Malaysians.

input_text: Malay weddings are the worst. So many pantang larang, the couple can't even enjoy their own wedding. What's the point?
reason: Dismisses Malay wedding customs as pointless, showing disrespect for cultural and religious traditions.
suggested_rewrite: Malay wedding traditions carry deep cultural and spiritual significance. While some customs may seem formal, they reflect important values of the community.

input_text: My uncle just sent this in family group: 'Don't eat at that mamak, they never wash hands properly.' Classic uncle move.
reason: Sharing a family elder's ethnically targeted food hygiene claim. 'Classic uncle move' is affectionate dismissal but still amplifies the stereotype about mamak restaurants.
suggested_rewrite: My uncle shared a food hygiene concern. Rather than targeting specific restaurants by type, we should check official health ratings and report genuine hygiene issues to authorities.

input_text: These delivery riders think they own the road lah. Almost kena langgar twice today at the zebra crossing. Idiots.
reason: Calls delivery riders idiots, escalating a pedestrian safety concern into personal insult.
suggested_rewrite: I had two close calls with delivery riders at the zebra crossing today. Better enforcement of traffic rules at crossings is needed for pedestrian safety.

input_text: These backpacker tourists treat Malaysia like their personal playground. Cheap beer, cheap food, and they leave a mess everywhere. Have some respect.
reason: Stereotypes backpacker tourists as disrespectful cheapskates, a xenophobic generalisation.
suggested_rewrite: Tourism brings economic benefits, but responsible tourism should be promoted. Clear guidelines for visitors on respecting local customs and cleanliness would be helpful.

input_text: Someone just posted the phone number of that rude cashier at the supermarket. I mean she was really rude but posting her number is a bit much. Then again, karma.
reason: Tacitly endorses doxxing a cashier's phone number by framing it as karma for rudeness.
suggested_rewrite: Rude service can be reported to the store management. Posting someone's personal phone number online is a privacy violation and could lead to harassment.

input_text: These so-called experts don't know what they're talking about. Total waste of taxpayer money.
reason: Dismissive criticism of government consultants, rude but within bounds of frustrated commentary.
suggested_rewrite: I question whether the appointed experts have the right qualifications for this task. Taxpayer-funded consultations should deliver meaningful results.

input_text: Someone forwarded this in WhatsApp — apparently foreign workers are getting free healthcare while Malaysians have to pay. Can anyone confirm?? This is crazy if true
reason: Amplifies an unverified WhatsApp rumour about healthcare access. The 'if true' framing lends credibility to potential misinformation that could fuel anti-foreigner sentiment.
suggested_rewrite: Received a WhatsApp message about foreign worker healthcare. Before sharing, let's verify with official sources like MOH. Spreading unverified claims can create unnecessary tension.

input_text: Tamil schools are holding back Indian kids. They come out and can't compete in the real world. Parents are wasting their children's future.
reason: Dismisses Tamil vernacular education as a hindrance, blaming parents for choosing mother-tongue education.
suggested_rewrite: Tamil schools can be strengthened with bilingual programs that maintain cultural identity while also preparing students for the broader job market.

input_text: The new condo development blocks our entire sea view. Developer didn't tell existing residents about the project. No transparency.
reason: New development blocking sea view with no prior notice to existing residents.
suggested_rewrite: New developments should undergo public consultation. Check if proper EIA and planning permissions were obtained and attend the local council's planning sessions.

input_text: My cousin is 33 and still not married. Every family gathering the aunties will ask her 'when ah?'. So toxic. Let people live their lives la
reason: While defending personal autonomy, labelling family elders' concern as 'toxic' is a strong characterisation that dismisses cultural norms around family. The sentiment is valid but could be expressed more diplomatically.
suggested_rewrite: My cousin faces a lot of pressure about marriage at family gatherings. While I understand the concern comes from love, it would be nicer if family could be more supportive of her timeline.

input_text: People who study art or music, then complain no job. Like hello, what did you expect? Should have picked something useful.
reason: Dismissive and condescending commentary about arts graduates' career prospects. Rude victim-blaming tone.
suggested_rewrite: Arts and music graduates face a challenging job market. More career guidance during university could help with expectations.

input_text: Lol the new minister's speech was like ChatGPT wrote it on low battery mode. Zero substance, all filler. Braincells sold separately I guess.
reason: Mocks a minister's speech as AI-generated garbage and jokes about missing brain cells. Creative but demeaning intelligence attack.
suggested_rewrite: The new minister's speech lacked depth and substance. Public addresses should contain clear policy positions and actionable plans.

input_text: JKR contractors always use the cheapest materials. Roads crack within months. This country will never develop if we keep this up.
reason: Makes sweeping claim about all JKR contractors and declares the country will never develop, cynical and demoralising.
suggested_rewrite: Quality control in government road construction projects needs to be strengthened. Independent inspections and contractor accountability clauses can ensure durable infrastructure.

input_text: Why do we even allow so many foreign workers? Every corner got Bangladeshi, Myanmar, Indonesian. Soon Malaysia won't even feel like Malaysia anymore
reason: Frames the presence of foreign workers as an existential threat to Malaysian identity. The alarmist 'won't feel like Malaysia' rhetoric promotes mild xenophobia.
suggested_rewrite: Malaysia has a high number of foreign workers in various sectors. A balanced policy that addresses labour needs while supporting local employment would benefit everyone.

input_text: KWSP says we need RM240k minimum to retire. Most Malaysians have less than RM50k. But sure, let's pretend the system is working.
reason: Cynical take on the retirement savings gap. Sarcastically implies the system is fundamentally broken without constructive engagement.
suggested_rewrite: The gap between recommended retirement savings and actual KWSP balances is concerning. Comprehensive strategies are needed to help Malaysians build adequate retirement funds.

input_text: Broadband coverage in rural Sabah is terrible. City people don't understand we still can't even load a webpage properly.
reason: Poor broadband coverage in rural Sabah creating digital divide.
suggested_rewrite: The digital divide in Sabah needs urgent attention under JENDELA. Report coverage gaps to MCMC and request satellite internet options through the KKDW program.

input_text: Imagine studying fine arts in 2025. Good luck working at Starbucks after graduation. Should have picked a real degree.
reason: Mocks fine arts students by implying they will end up in low-paying service jobs, dismissing creative education.
suggested_rewrite: Fine arts graduates can pursue diverse careers in design, advertising, education, and the creative economy. Every field has its own merits.

input_text: After-school tuition has become mandatory for Malaysian kids. If don't go tuition, cannot keep up. System failed.
reason: Tuition dependency reflecting education system failure, widespread concern.
suggested_rewrite: The reliance on private tuition suggests classroom teaching may need enhancement. Education reforms should focus on improving in-school learning outcomes.

input_text: Got scammed on Carousell by a seller with a Malay name. RM200 gone just like that. Tried to report but the platform doesn't care. Some sellers really have no integrity
reason: Unnecessarily identifies the scammer by ethnic name, which implies a racial dimension to the fraud. The 'no integrity' comment, combined with the ethnic identifier, could be read as stereotyping.
suggested_rewrite: Got scammed by a seller on Carousell — lost RM200. Reported to the platform but no response yet. Always use the platform's secure payment option to protect yourself.

input_text: That new housing minister ah, I don't think he even knows what a B40 flat looks like. IQ probably lower than the flat number.
reason: Mocks the housing minister's intelligence and implies they are disconnected from B40 reality. Personal attack.
suggested_rewrite: The housing minister should visit B40 communities to better understand their challenges before making policy decisions.

input_text: This clinic prescribes antibiotics for everything. Common cold also give antibiotics. Over-prescription is dangerous.
reason: Over-prescription of antibiotics for minor ailments, public health concern.
suggested_rewrite: Antibiotic overuse contributes to resistance. Patients can politely ask doctors if antibiotics are truly necessary for their condition.

input_text: If you've never tasted real Penang laksa from a hawker who's been cooking for 30 years, your opinion on laksa means nothing.
reason: Dismisses anyone without specific Penang hawker experience from having a valid opinion on laksa. Elitist food gatekeeping.
suggested_rewrite: Penang laksa from veteran hawkers is a benchmark worth trying. Everyone's taste preferences are valid, though experiencing the original helps appreciate the dish.

input_text: Auntie at kopitiam scold me for sitting too long. Hello, I paid for my drink already la. Customer always wrong in Malaysia is it?
reason: Rude complaint about kopitiam auntie with sarcastic generalization about Malaysian service culture.
suggested_rewrite: A kopitiam vendor asked me to leave after finishing my drink. Clear seating policies would help avoid misunderstandings.

input_text: MyDebit card reader at petrol station always broken. Force people to pay cash. Very inconvenient in cashless era.
reason: Broken card readers at petrol stations undermining cashless payment adoption.
suggested_rewrite: Petrol stations should maintain working payment terminals. Report non-functional card readers to the station management and Bank Negara if persistent.

input_text: Tabung Haji managed to lose billions in bad investments and nobody went to jail. If that's not Malaysia Boleh, I don't know what is.
reason: Cynical commentary using 'Malaysia Boleh' sarcastically to criticise lack of accountability in Tabung Haji losses.
suggested_rewrite: The investment losses at Tabung Haji raise serious accountability questions. Depositors deserve to know what measures are being taken to prevent future losses.

input_text: Real estate agent show me different unit from what I signed for. When I complained, they said it's 'similar'. Total scam.
reason: Real estate bait-and-switch with different unit from signed agreement.
suggested_rewrite: If the unit differs from what was agreed in writing, you have grounds for a complaint. Consult the Board of Valuers, Estate Agents and Property Managers.

input_text: People who use public transport in KL just cannot afford a car, simple as that
reason: Classist generalization equating public transport use exclusively with inability to afford a car.
suggested_rewrite: Many people use public transport in KL for convenience, environmental reasons, or to avoid traffic congestion.

input_text: The pedestrian crossing light only gives 10 seconds to cross a 6-lane road. Elderly people can't make it. Dangerous design.
reason: Inadequate pedestrian crossing time on wide road endangering elderly, safety concern.
suggested_rewrite: Pedestrian crossing timings should account for elderly and disabled users. Report unsafe crossings to the local traffic authority for timing adjustments.

input_text: Went to renew my passport and wasted my entire day. The JPJ staff move at snail pace. No wonder people hate dealing with government agencies.
reason: Mocks government staff as snail-paced and generalises hatred of agencies from one experience.
suggested_rewrite: Long wait times for passport renewals can be frustrating. Online appointment systems and more efficient processes would improve the experience for everyone.

input_text: Tamil school students still studying in dilapidated buildings. Ceiling collapsed last week. When will someone take action?
reason: Tamil school structural safety crisis with ceiling collapse, delayed action.
suggested_rewrite: Student safety is paramount. Schools with structural failures should be immediately assessed and temporary facilities provided while repairs are done.

input_text: HR at my company is a total waste of space. They only exist to protect the company, not the employees. Don't even bother complaining to them.
reason: Calls HR a waste of space and discourages others from using proper channels, dismissive and unhelpful.
suggested_rewrite: HR departments should serve both company and employee interests. If internal complaints aren't addressed, employees can seek guidance from the Labour Department.

input_text: Condo management should just ban cooking strong smelling food. We all know which community I'm talking about.
reason: Dog-whistle targeting a specific ethnic community over cooking smells while pretending not to name them. Coded communal hostility.
suggested_rewrite: Condo management could improve ventilation systems and establish cooking guidelines that apply equally to all residents.

input_text: Grab driver cancel my ride 5 times in a row because my destination is 'too near'. Then surge pricing kicks in. System rigged.
reason: Multiple ride cancellations for short trips leading to surge pricing, unfair system.
suggested_rewrite: Ride cancellations for short trips should be addressed by the platform. Report repeated cancellations through the app's feedback system.

input_text: Local councillors are honestly the most useless public servants. Can't even fix a pothole but expect us to pay taxes. Scam artists.
reason: Calls councillors scam artists and useless, an insulting generalisation of public servants.
suggested_rewrite: Residents expect basic maintenance like pothole repair from their local councils. Better reporting mechanisms and accountability would improve service delivery.

input_text: Heard from WhatsApp group that a minister's son got caught with drugs but case kena tutup. Typical la Malaysia.
reason: Spreads an unverified WhatsApp rumor about a politician's family while asserting it as typical behavior, amplifying distrust.
suggested_rewrite: Unverified claims from messaging groups should be treated with caution. If there is credible evidence of a cover-up, it should be reported to the relevant authorities.

input_text: The new LRT extension is the biggest waste of taxpayer money. Who even uses public transport in Malaysia? Only people who can't afford cars.
reason: Stigmatises public transport users as poor and dismisses infrastructure investment cynically.
suggested_rewrite: Public transport investment should be matched with better connectivity and service quality to increase ridership across all income groups.

input_text: The flood relief center has no proper food or bedding. Victims sleeping on the floor with just a blanket. This is how we treat displaced people?
reason: Inadequate flood relief center conditions, questioning treatment of disaster victims.
suggested_rewrite: Flood relief centers should meet minimum standards for food, bedding, and hygiene. Volunteer organizations and government agencies should coordinate better preparedness.

input_text: Mental health is just an excuse for lazy people who don't want to work hard
reason: Dismissing mental health as an excuse for laziness. Harmful misinformation that stigmatizes those with genuine conditions.
suggested_rewrite: Mental health is a real medical concern. People struggling with it need support, not judgment.

input_text: Landlord didn't return my deposit even after 3 months. Keep giving excuses. No proper rental law to protect tenants.
reason: Landlord withholding deposit for 3 months with excuses, no tenant protection law.
suggested_rewrite: Document all deposit communications. If the landlord refuses to return the deposit, consider filing a claim at the Small Claims Court for amounts up to RM5,000.

input_text: Disabled parking always taken by non-disabled drivers. Blue badge required but no one checks. Shameful behavior.
reason: Non-disabled drivers taking disabled parking with no enforcement of blue badge.
suggested_rewrite: OKU parking enforcement should be taken seriously. Report misuse to the local council and advocate for proper monitoring systems.

input_text: These Bangla workers loitering outside the shop every evening, making people uncomfortable. Someone should call immigration on them.
reason: Targets Bangladeshi workers with xenophobic language, assuming they are illegal and calling for enforcement action based on their presence alone.
suggested_rewrite: Public spaces belong to everyone. If there are genuine safety concerns about loitering, they can be raised with local authorities without targeting specific nationalities.

input_text: Condo gym equipment all rusty and broken. Swimming pool green color. But maintenance fee goes up every year.
reason: Deteriorating condo facilities despite rising maintenance fees, management accountability.
suggested_rewrite: Request a detailed maintenance expenditure report at the next AGM. Residents can vote to change the management company if service remains poor.

input_text: Local councils are just collecting assessment tax and doing nothing. Roads full of potholes, drains clogged. Where does the money go?
reason: Accuses local councils of misusing public funds with a dismissive tone instead of using formal accountability mechanisms.
suggested_rewrite: Local councils should be more transparent about how assessment tax revenue is allocated. Residents can request audited financial reports and attend council meetings to raise concerns.

input_text: Government hospital cancel my appointment three times already. Each time have to take leave from work. So inconsiderate.
reason: Triple appointment cancellation at government hospital causing work disruption.
suggested_rewrite: Frequent appointment cancellations waste patients' time and work leave. Hospitals should provide adequate notice and priority rescheduling for cancelled appointments.

input_text: Every Deepavali sure la the whole taman got noise until midnight. Fireworks somemore. Other people don't celebrate also kena tahan.
reason: Eye-rolling about Deepavali celebrations with 'other people' framing that creates communal divide around religious festivities.
suggested_rewrite: Deepavali fireworks can be noisy late at night. Community guidelines on celebration hours would help everyone enjoy the festival responsibly.

input_text: Delivery riders think traffic rules don't apply to them. One day they'll learn the hard way and drag innocent people with them.
reason: Generalizes delivery riders as reckless and ominously predicts they'll hurt innocents. Threatening undertone toward gig workers.
suggested_rewrite: Road safety is a shared responsibility. Better training and enforcement can help delivery riders and other road users stay safe.

input_text: Online shopping in Malaysia is a scam. Half the products are fake and sellers just disappear after taking your money.
reason: Sweeping generalization about online shopping being a scam, frustrated consumer.
suggested_rewrite: Online shopping fraud is a concern. Buyers should use verified sellers, check reviews, and report scams to KPDNHEP or the platform's dispute center.

input_text: Pediatric ward in government hospital is overcrowded. Two kids sharing one bed. This is not acceptable.
reason: Overcrowded pediatric ward complaint about children sharing hospital beds.
suggested_rewrite: Overcrowding in pediatric wards is a patient safety concern. Hospital administrations need more beds and resources to handle patient volumes.

input_text: The Malay special rights debate is so tired. Other races built this country too but we always get treated like outsiders.
reason: Frames the special rights debate as exclusionary toward non-Malays in a way that could inflame communal tensions.
suggested_rewrite: All Malaysians have contributed to the nation's development. A constructive dialogue about inclusive policies that uplift all communities is needed.

input_text: I can't believe they raised the toll price again. Highway robbery literally. Government doesn't care about middle class at all.
reason: Angry toll price complaint with pun ('highway robbery'), populist frustration about government priorities.
suggested_rewrite: The toll increase will impact daily commuters significantly. I hope the government considers alternative financing models or public transport improvements.

input_text: Bro you're 31 and still playing video games and living with your parents? Time to grow up la. No wonder cannot find girlfriend also
reason: Shames someone for their lifestyle choices and living situation, linking hobbies to relationship failure. The 'grow up' framing is judgmental and dismissive.
suggested_rewrite: Everyone has different life paths and hobbies. What matters is being happy and working towards your own goals, whatever they may be.

input_text: Colleague from China always talk so loud in office. I know la your culture like that but can you respect others or not? This is Malaysia bro.
reason: Stereotyping someone from China as loud due to their culture, dismissive and othering tone.
suggested_rewrite: My colleague tends to speak quite loudly in the office. I should find a polite way to discuss office noise levels.

input_text: I bet Syabas executives drink imported mineral water at home. They know better than anyone what's in our tap water.
reason: Insinuates water company executives avoid their own product due to contamination. Conspiratorial implication without evidence.
suggested_rewrite: Concerns about tap water quality are valid. Independent testing and transparent reporting by water companies would help build public confidence.

input_text: Every day stuck in traffic jam for 2 hours. My life is just work, traffic, sleep. Malaysian dream right?
reason: Sarcastic commentary on work-life balance ruined by traffic congestion.
suggested_rewrite: Long commutes affect quality of life significantly. Flexible work arrangements and better public transport could help Malaysian workers reclaim their time.

input_text: Still single at 38? Something must be really wrong with you la. Even the weird kids from school are married already.
reason: Aggressively age-shames someone for being single at 38 and implies personal deficiency.
suggested_rewrite: Being single at any age is a personal choice. Everyone has their own timeline for relationships and life milestones.

input_text: Mamak restaurant charge RM12 for roti canai set now? They think they fine dining ke
reason: Sarcastic dramatic reaction to mamak restaurant pricing. Dismissive and contemptuous.
suggested_rewrite: Mamak restaurant prices have increased significantly. The roti canai set at RM12 seems overpriced for many customers.

input_text: The way some of these politicians talk, you'd think they never went to school. Total clowns running the country.
reason: Calling politicians 'clowns' and implying they are uneducated goes beyond policy critique into personal contempt and ridicule.
suggested_rewrite: Some politicians' public statements don't reflect the seriousness of their role. I expect better communication and professionalism from our leaders.

input_text: Malaysian politicians are all clowns. Left wing, right wing, same circus. Don't waste your vote on any of them.
reason: Dismisses the entire political spectrum as a circus and discourages voting, undermining democratic participation.
suggested_rewrite: While frustration with political parties is understandable, exercising your right to vote remains important. Research candidates' track records before making your choice.

input_text: Honestly, the whole 'special rights' thing is just legal discrimination at this point. Change my mind.
reason: Dismissing constitutional provisions as 'legal discrimination' without nuance. Legitimate policy debate but phrased provocatively in a way that could inflame intercommunal tensions.
suggested_rewrite: I think it's worth having an open, respectful discussion about affirmative action policies and whether they still serve their original purpose effectively.

input_text: Wedding venue charge exorbitant price during peak season. RM30k for a basic hall. Young couples how to afford?
reason: Wedding venue price gouging during peak season, affordability concern.
suggested_rewrite: Consider off-peak dates, community halls, or outdoor venues for more affordable wedding options. Budget planning tools can help couples manage costs.

input_text: These Grab drivers, mostly Malay guys, always take the long route to charge more. Cannot trust them.
reason: Racially profiles Malay Grab drivers as dishonest. Generalises an ethnic group based on isolated negative experiences.
suggested_rewrite: If a driver takes a longer route, report it through the app. Route issues happen with drivers of all backgrounds and can usually be resolved through the platform.

input_text: My Grab account got hacked and someone used my credit card for multiple rides. Customer service took 2 weeks to respond.
reason: Ride-hailing account hack with credit card fraud and slow customer service response.
suggested_rewrite: Immediately freeze your credit card if your ride-hailing account is hacked. File a police report and contact your bank in addition to the app's support.

input_text: This condo management is a joke la. Maintenance fee go up but lift still broken for 2 weeks. Where the money go?
reason: Sarcastic attack on condo management implying misuse of funds. Rude but rooted in genuine frustration.
suggested_rewrite: The condo maintenance fee increased but the lift has been broken for 2 weeks. Management should provide transparency on spending.

input_text: I saw this being shared around: 'Hindu temples are getting too loud, disturbing the peace.' Thoughts?
reason: Amplifying a complaint targeting Hindu worship noise specifically rather than general noise ordinance issues. Framing as 'thoughts?' invites pile-on rather than resolution.
suggested_rewrite: There are occasional noise concerns near places of worship. These should be resolved through local council mediation that respects both religious practice and residential needs.

input_text: If you didn't grow up eating roti canai from a mamak, you have no right to judge whose roti canai is best.
reason: Gates roti canai appreciation to those with specific cultural upbringing. Excludes newcomers from food discourse.
suggested_rewrite: Growing up with mamak roti canai gives a unique perspective, but everyone can appreciate and share their food preferences respectfully.

input_text: The queue at the clinic was insane and the nurse behind the counter had zero urgency. Chit chatting like nobody waiting.
reason: Rude criticism of clinic nurse for chatting during busy hours. Frustrated patient with dismissive tone.
suggested_rewrite: The clinic was very busy today and service was slow. I hope staffing can be improved during peak hours.

input_text: GLC companies are just welfare programs for connected Malays. Meritocracy doesn't exist in this country.
reason: Racializes criticism of GLCs by targeting Malays specifically, framing affirmative action as racial favouritism.
suggested_rewrite: GLC governance and hiring practices should prioritize competence and transparency. Meritocratic reforms can strengthen both performance and public confidence.

input_text: OKU toilet always locked or used as store room. But seriously la kan, is it really that many OKU using public toilet?
reason: Acknowledges OKU toilet misuse but then dismissively questions the actual need for disability-accessible toilets.
suggested_rewrite: OKU toilets are often misused as storerooms. These facilities must be kept accessible for persons with disabilities who need them.

input_text: Service at the immigration office is terrible. Rude staff, long wait, and they still can't get your name right on the passport.
reason: Multiple complaints about immigration office service - rudeness, delays, errors. Harsh but focused on service quality.
suggested_rewrite: The immigration office experience needs improvement in staff courtesy, wait times, and data accuracy. An efficient appointment system would help significantly.

input_text: No public phone charging stations in this city. Everyone depends on phone but when battery dies, you're stuck.
reason: Lack of public phone charging infrastructure in a smartphone-dependent society.
suggested_rewrite: Public charging stations in high-traffic areas would be a useful modern amenity. Carry a portable charger as a personal backup.

input_text: BTO flats in Singapore so affordable compared to Malaysia. Our government can't even build proper affordable housing. So embarrassing. No wonder people migrate
reason: Uses unfavourable comparison with Singapore to shame Malaysian housing policy. While the comparison may have merit, calling it 'embarrassing' and linking it to migration is emotionally charged.
suggested_rewrite: Singapore's BTO model offers lessons for affordable housing. Malaysia could explore similar approaches to make homeownership more accessible for young Malaysians.

input_text: Bro that influencer is literally the dumbest person I've ever seen. How does she even have followers? Brain-dead content.
reason: Excessive personal insults toward a public figure — 'dumbest', 'brain-dead' — crossing from content critique into personal attack.
suggested_rewrite: I don't find that influencer's content valuable or well-researched. I'm surprised by the large following given the quality.

input_text: Disable people want ramp everywhere, special toilet, special parking. Everything also special treatment. Other people also got needs what.
reason: Dismissive and resentful view of disability accommodation, framing accessibility as unfair special treatment.
suggested_rewrite: Accessibility features like ramps and OKU parking benefit everyone, including elderly and injured persons. Inclusivity is important.

input_text: Why la so many people want to be YouTuber. Get a real job la. Not everyone can be famous, some of you are just cringe.
reason: Dismissive and shaming of people pursuing content creation. Condescending judgment of career choices.
suggested_rewrite: Content creation is competitive and not everyone will succeed. It's important to have a backup plan while pursuing creative careers.

input_text: Mosquito fogging only happens when dengue becomes serious. Why not do it regularly? Prevention better than cure right?
reason: Reactive vs proactive dengue prevention criticism, public health complaint.
suggested_rewrite: Regular mosquito fogging schedules would help prevent dengue outbreaks. Residents can also help by eliminating stagnant water in their compounds.

input_text: My Indian colleague always come late to meeting but when boss ask, sure got excuse ready. Very talented in that area la I tell you.
reason: Singling out a colleague by ethnicity for a workplace complaint, implying the behaviour is linked to being Indian.
suggested_rewrite: A colleague of mine is frequently late to meetings. It affects team productivity and should be addressed by management.

input_text: My elderly father's wheelchair ramp at the government building is too steep. He almost fell backwards. ADA compliance is a joke here.
reason: Government building wheelchair ramp dangerously steep, accessibility compliance failure.
suggested_rewrite: Wheelchair ramps must meet Malaysian Standards MS 1184. Report non-compliant ramps at government buildings to JKR and the Disability Rights Division.

input_text: This childcare center has too many kids per caregiver. Safety ratio completely ignored. JPNIN should inspect more often.
reason: Childcare center exceeding safe child-to-caregiver ratio, inspection complaint.
suggested_rewrite: Childcare centers must maintain proper child-to-caregiver ratios. Report overcrowding concerns to JPNIN for inspection and enforcement.

input_text: Took my car for inspection, they failed it for minor scratch. But lorries with bald tires on the road no problem. Double standard.
reason: Vehicle inspection inconsistency frustration comparing cars to uninspected trucks.
suggested_rewrite: Vehicle inspection standards should be applied consistently to all vehicle types. Report unsafe commercial vehicles to JPJ for enforcement.

input_text: Got message in group: 'Dengue cases all from foreign worker areas. They don't clean up.' Forward to create awareness!
reason: Forwarding unverified claim linking dengue exclusively to foreign worker areas and calling for virality. Creates ethnic blame for a public health issue.
suggested_rewrite: Dengue prevention requires community-wide effort. Check KKM's official dengue hotspot data rather than blaming specific communities. Everyone should eliminate standing water around their homes.

input_text: The road conditions here are ridiculous. Potholes everywhere. Does the council even exist?
reason: Frustrated road quality complaint questioning council's existence, hyperbolic but legitimate concern.
suggested_rewrite: The road conditions in our area need urgent attention. I plan to submit a formal complaint to the local council with photos of the potholes.

input_text: The amount of litter on Malaysian beaches is shameful. Tourism campaigns but can't even keep beaches clean.
reason: Beach pollution frustration contrasting with tourism promotion efforts.
suggested_rewrite: Maintaining clean beaches is essential for both tourism and marine ecosystems. Regular beach cleanups and stricter littering enforcement would help.

input_text: Got so many potholes on this road. Already two motorcyclists fell this week. Waiting for someone to die before fixing ah?
reason: Dangerous potholes causing motorcycle accidents, dramatic frustration.
suggested_rewrite: Potholes causing accidents are an urgent safety issue. Report the exact location to JKR and your local councilor for immediate repair.

input_text: Our tap water literally smells like chlorine and they tell us it's safe. The water companies are gaslighting the entire nation.
reason: Accuses water companies of gaslighting the public about water safety. Sensationalises a legitimate concern into a conspiracy narrative.
suggested_rewrite: Chlorine odour in tap water can be concerning. Residents can request water quality reports and raise issues with their local water authority.

input_text: Satire page posted: 'Breaking: Minister discovers rakyat exists. Promises to Google what poverty means.' Too real.
reason: Satirical content mocking a minister's disconnection from the public. While satire is protected speech, the specific framing implies incompetence and callous disregard, crossing into personal attack.
suggested_rewrite: It's important that our ministers understand the daily struggles of ordinary Malaysians. More grassroots engagement would help bridge this perception gap.

input_text: Local politicians only show up during election time. Rest of the year they're invisible. Such hypocrites.
reason: Cynical political observation calling politicians hypocrites, common democratic criticism in sharp tone.
suggested_rewrite: It's frustrating when elected representatives aren't visible between elections. Voters should hold their representatives accountable through regular engagement and feedback.

input_text: Malaysian schools are honestly garbage. No wonder our students can't compete internationally. The whole education system is a failure.
reason: Labels entire education system as garbage and a failure, a sweeping and dismissive attack.
suggested_rewrite: Malaysia's education system faces challenges in international competitiveness. Targeted reforms in curriculum and teacher training could help improve student outcomes.

input_text: My dad said: 'Last time this area was safe, now all these Rohingya come in and everything changed.' He's not wrong though...
reason: Endorsing a parent's claim that Rohingya refugees caused safety decline in an area. 'He's not wrong though' explicitly validates the ethnic blame.
suggested_rewrite: My dad has safety concerns about our area. Rather than blaming any particular community, we should work with local authorities to address specific safety issues.

input_text: The food at this famous restaurant is overrated. Taste so-so only but price like hotel. Don't know why people queue up.
reason: Dismissive food review of a popular restaurant, opinionated and slightly rude but standard consumer opinion.
suggested_rewrite: I found the food at this restaurant didn't quite match its reputation or pricing. Perhaps others enjoy specific dishes that I didn't try.

input_text: Malaysia's school system is obsessed with controlling students. Hair length, sock colour, belt type. It's about obedience, not education.
reason: Frames entire Malaysian school system as authoritarian control. Sweeping dismissal that could undermine public education institutions.
suggested_rewrite: While discipline has its place, schools could benefit from reviewing whether all uniform rules contribute meaningfully to the learning environment.

input_text: Community hall in our area always locked. Want to use for events also cannot. What's the point of having it?
reason: Community hall always locked and unavailable for residents' use.
suggested_rewrite: Community halls should be accessible to residents with a simple booking system. Check with the local council or residents' association about access procedures.

input_text: New housing area but no bus route passes through. Everyone needs a car. What happened to sustainable planning?
reason: New development without public transport access forcing car dependency.
suggested_rewrite: Urban development should include public transport connectivity from the planning stage. Petition the transport authority for bus route extensions to new areas.

input_text: The kopitiam uncle increased my tea tarik price from RM1.80 to RM2.50 overnight. No warning, no reason given.
reason: Overnight 39% price increase on tea tarik with no explanation.
suggested_rewrite: Sudden price increases can be frustrating. Ask the vendor about the reason - it may be related to ingredient cost increases.

input_text: Why do we need to give scholarships to foreign students? Our own Malaysian students also struggling. Country first la.
reason: Mild xenophobic framing of foreign student scholarships as zero-sum. The 'country first' rhetoric is mildly polarising.
suggested_rewrite: It's worth examining how scholarship allocation balances supporting local students and attracting international talent that benefits our universities.

input_text: I need to go to three different counters to settle one bill. Government efficiency at its finest.
reason: Sarcastic criticism of government requiring multiple counters for one transaction.
suggested_rewrite: Multi-counter processes could be streamlined through integrated systems. Many government payments can now be made online through MyGovernment portal.

input_text: My car warranty void because I didn't service at authorized center. But nearest authorized center is 100km away. Unfair policy.
reason: Car warranty voided due to distance to authorized service center, access issue.
suggested_rewrite: Warranty terms requiring authorized centers should consider geographic accessibility. Raise this with the manufacturer and consider KPDNHEP if the policy is unreasonable.

input_text: Parking attendant at the mall was so rude to me just because I asked for directions. These people act like they own the place. Zero customer service skills.
reason: Rude generalisation about parking attendants, 'these people' is dismissive and demeaning towards service workers.
suggested_rewrite: The parking attendant at the mall wasn't very helpful when I asked for directions. Better training would improve the customer experience.

input_text: The pedestrian bridge near my house has no roof and no lighting. During rain and at night, nobody uses it because it's unsafe.
reason: Pedestrian bridge without roof or lighting making it unusable in rain or at night.
suggested_rewrite: Pedestrian bridges should include basic amenities like roofing and lighting. Request upgrades through your local councilor's office.

input_text: Why do Indian guys always rev their motorcycle so loud? Whole neighborhood can hear. Compensation for something is it? So inconsiderate.
reason: Ethnic targeting of Indian men for motorcycle noise with innuendo ('compensation for something'), crude and stereotyping.
suggested_rewrite: Loud motorcycle exhaust noise at night disturbs the neighbourhood. Noise regulations should be enforced regardless of who the rider is.

input_text: I think our Transport Minister got his degree from clown school la. Every policy he announce also make no sense.
reason: Mocks the Transport Minister's education and intelligence. Sarcastic personal attack on public figure.
suggested_rewrite: The Transport Minister's recent policies have been poorly received. More research-backed approaches would improve public confidence.

input_text: My cousin wants to be a musician. I told him straight - there's no future in music in Malaysia. Get a proper job la.
reason: Dismisses music as having no future in Malaysia, pressuring someone away from their passion.
suggested_rewrite: The music industry in Malaysia is growing with streaming platforms and live events. It's important to support musicians while also planning financially.

input_text: Just saw a TikTok about how foreign workers are taking over Chow Kit. Is this true?? If yes then what is the government doing about it??
reason: Amplifies unverified TikTok claims about foreigners 'taking over' an area. The 'is this true?' framing gives it plausibility while the alarmist language frames it as a crisis.
suggested_rewrite: Saw a TikTok about demographic changes in Chow Kit. Does anyone have reliable data on this? Would be good to understand the actual situation before jumping to conclusions.

input_text: This factory emitting thick black smoke every night after inspection hours. Clearly hiding their pollution from inspectors.
reason: Factory timing pollution for after inspection hours, deliberate environmental violation.
suggested_rewrite: Document night emissions with photos, timestamps, and video. Report to DOE and request unannounced nighttime inspections of the facility.

input_text: Penang drivers are the worst in Malaysia and that's saying something. Every lane is their lane, every signal is optional.
reason: Regional stereotyping of Penang drivers. Generalises all drivers from one state negatively.
suggested_rewrite: Traffic behaviour in Penang can be challenging for visitors. Better enforcement and driver education could improve road safety across Malaysia.

input_text: Old people should just stop using smartphones if they can't figure it out. Always blocking the queue at the bank trying to use the app.
reason: Ageist remark dismissing older adults' right to use technology, showing impatience and contempt for their learning curve.
suggested_rewrite: Banks should provide more staff assistance for customers who need help with digital services, especially older adults adapting to new technology.

input_text: Festival noise in Malaysia is out of control. Speakers blasting religious songs at 5am and nobody dares complain because 'sensitivity'. Ridiculous.
reason: Dismisses religious observance as mere noise and mocks sensitivity concerns, potentially provocative.
suggested_rewrite: Balancing religious observance with community peace is important. Local authorities can work with religious communities to set reasonable guidelines on sound levels and timing.

input_text: Can't even use the public library because it's always closed or under renovation. Where do students go to study?
reason: Frustrated about public library closures affecting students' study options.
suggested_rewrite: Consistent public library hours are essential for students. The local council should provide alternative study spaces during renovation periods.

input_text: Senior citizens on smartphones so clueless, always asking how to use WhatsApp again and again
reason: Ageist mockery of elderly people's digital literacy. Condescending and dismissive of their effort to learn.
suggested_rewrite: Many seniors are learning to use smartphones. Patient guidance can help them build confidence with technology.

input_text: These Tan Sri businessmen always crying about not enough skilled workers tapi gaji yang offer tu macam gaji 1990s. Hypocrites.
reason: Calls titled business owners hypocrites for low wages. While raising valid wage concerns, the personal attack and generalization are inflammatory.
suggested_rewrite: The skills gap discussion should include competitive wages. Employers offering market-rate salaries are more likely to attract and retain talented workers.

input_text: Some parents really need to learn how to control their kids in public. Running around, screaming - no manners taught at all.
reason: Judgmental parenting critique about children's behavior in public, frustrated social observation.
suggested_rewrite: It can be challenging for parents to manage active children in public spaces. Perhaps more family-friendly areas would help everyone enjoy public spaces better.

input_text: The school bus driver always late. Kids standing in the sun for 30 minutes every day. Complain to school also no action.
reason: School bus tardiness exposing children to heat, school unresponsive to complaints.
suggested_rewrite: School bus punctuality affects children's wellbeing. Parents should document delays and raise the issue formally with the school board and bus operator.

input_text: Bus schedule here is so unreliable. Wait for 45 minutes then three buses come at once. Ridiculous management.
reason: Frustrated public transport reliability complaint, common urban commuter frustration.
suggested_rewrite: Bus scheduling in our area needs significant improvement. Real-time tracking apps and better fleet management could help resolve the bunching problem.

input_text: That MP's press conference was so embarrassing. Can't even answer simple questions. Macam budak tadika yang kena soal.
reason: Mocks an MP with a childish comparison rather than substantively critiquing their policy position.
suggested_rewrite: Elected representatives should be well-prepared to address public questions. Media training and better briefings could improve communication with the public.

input_text: Insurance claim for flood damage took 6 months to process. By that time, secondary mold damage cost more than the original flood.
reason: 6-month insurance claim processing for flood causing secondary mold damage exceeding original loss.
suggested_rewrite: Long claim delays can be escalated through BNM's complaints process. Document all secondary damage with photos and receipts to include in your claim.

input_text: Food delivery apps charge 30% commission from small restaurants. How can small businesses survive? These apps are predatory.
reason: Delivery app commission rates hurting small restaurant businesses, predatory accusation.
suggested_rewrite: High delivery app commissions affect small business viability. Restaurant owners can explore alternatives like direct ordering systems or negotiate better commission rates.

input_text: Younger generation thinks changing job every year is normal. No loyalty, no patience. Then complain no one wants to hire them. Padan muka.
reason: Dismissive generalisation of younger workers, using 'padan muka' (serves them right) in a mocking tone.
suggested_rewrite: Job-hopping is more common now. Both employers and employees could benefit from understanding each other's expectations better.

input_text: Genuine question: why do Malay weddings have to block the entire road? Other races don't do that.
reason: Framing a logistical complaint as a racial comparison, singling out Malay wedding customs and implying other races are more considerate. Presents stereotype as fact.
suggested_rewrite: Road closures for events can be inconvenient. It would help if event organizers coordinate with local authorities to minimize traffic disruption regardless of the occasion.

input_text: Strata title for my apartment still not issued after 10 years. Developer ghosted. What protection do buyers have?
reason: 10-year wait for strata title with unresponsive developer, buyer protection concern.
suggested_rewrite: Developers are legally required to issue strata titles within a timeframe. File a complaint with the Commissioner of Buildings and consider legal action through the Housing Tribunal.

input_text: The recycling bins in my area are always contaminated with regular trash. Recycling efforts wasted because nobody educates residents.
reason: Recycling bins contaminated by regular trash due to lack of resident education.
suggested_rewrite: Effective recycling requires community education. Suggest your RA organize workshops and use clear signage on bins showing what goes where.

input_text: So hard to find halal food options in East Malaysia compared to West Malaysia. Feels like we're forgotten.
reason: Limited halal food options in East Malaysia, feeling neglected as Muslim.
suggested_rewrite: Expanding halal certification support to East Malaysia would help Muslim consumers and food businesses. JAKIM can work with state authorities to increase certified outlets.

input_text: Sarawak people so naive one, always kena tipu by politicians promising development
reason: Calling Sarawak people naive and easily deceived. Regional stereotyping that is condescending and patronizing.
suggested_rewrite: Sarawak voters deserve genuine follow-through on development promises from their elected representatives.

input_text: Chinese girls on dating apps always ask 'what car you drive' within the first 5 messages. It's like a job interview, not a date.
reason: Ethnic and gender stereotyping of Chinese women as materialistic in dating scenarios.
suggested_rewrite: Some people on dating apps prioritize financial status early on. Everyone has different priorities when looking for a partner.

input_text: Rented a room in SS2 from this Chinese aunty. She charges RM900 for a tiny room and won't even fix the aircon. These landlords only care about money, nothing else
reason: Identifies the landlord by ethnicity and then makes a sweeping generalisation about 'these landlords' caring only about money. Links greed to ethnic identity.
suggested_rewrite: My landlord in SS2 charges RM900 for a small room and refuses to fix the aircon. Tenants should know their rights — landlords are obligated to maintain the property.

input_text: RM15 for teh tarik? This country is going to the dogs seriously
reason: Overly dramatic reaction to price increase with dismissive national-level condemnation.
suggested_rewrite: RM15 for teh tarik is extremely overpriced. Rising food costs need to be addressed by regulators.

input_text: Another day, another corruption scandal. At this point I'm not even surprised. The whole system is rotten from top to bottom.
reason: Sweeping dismissal of the entire governance system as corrupt, promoting cynicism without acknowledging anti-corruption efforts.
suggested_rewrite: Repeated corruption cases are concerning. Supporting anti-corruption bodies and demanding greater transparency can help drive systemic improvement.

input_text: The grab driver was so rude, scolding me for the pickup location. These drivers think they're doing us a favour.
reason: Frustrated ride-hailing complaint generalizing about driver rudeness.
suggested_rewrite: I had a negative experience with a ride-hailing driver's attitude. I'll rate the trip accordingly and provide specific feedback through the app.

input_text: My neighbor's kid got into that school because daddy donated money. Meritocracy my foot
reason: Nepotism accusation targeting a specific child and parent. Rude dismissal of the child's potential merit.
suggested_rewrite: School admissions should be transparent and merit-based so all students have a fair chance.

input_text: Festival season again and my whole neighbourhood sounds like a war zone. Can these people not celebrate without destroying everyone else's peace?
reason: Uses 'these people' to single out a community's festive celebration, dismissive of cultural traditions.
suggested_rewrite: Festival celebrations are important culturally, but loud noise late at night can affect neighbours. Mutual understanding and agreed quiet hours would benefit everyone.

input_text: Hospital food so bad that patients prefer to starve. Recovery delayed because of poor nutrition. Management please take note.
reason: Hospital food quality so poor patients prefer not eating, affecting recovery.
suggested_rewrite: Hospital nutrition is critical for patient recovery. Provide specific feedback to the hospital's dietitian department and patient affairs unit.

input_text: Some hawkers really have no hygiene standards. How can health inspectors allow them to operate?
reason: Questioning food hygiene standards and enforcement, blunt consumer health concern.
suggested_rewrite: I've noticed some hawker stalls could improve their hygiene practices. More regular health inspections would benefit both vendors and consumers.

input_text: Every Malaysian Chinese sure go JB buy groceries one, kiasu mentality never change
reason: Ethnic stereotyping of Malaysian Chinese as kiasu. Applies a negative trait to an entire ethnic group.
suggested_rewrite: Many Malaysians cross to JB for more affordable groceries. It's a practical choice regardless of background.

input_text: Nobody maintains the monsoon drain near my house. Kids play near it. One day someone will fall in and drown.
reason: Unmaintained monsoon drain near homes posing drowning risk for children.
suggested_rewrite: Unmaintained monsoon drains are drowning hazards. Report to JPS and request safety fencing. Parents should keep children away from open drains.

input_text: Datang JPJ nak settle saman, the officer literally rolling eyes at me. Government workers and their attitude problem, I swear.
reason: Generalises all government workers as having attitude problems based on one encounter.
suggested_rewrite: Customer service training for frontline government staff can significantly improve public experience. Feedback channels should be easily accessible for residents to report both positive and negative interactions.

input_text: These fresh grads want RM4k starting salary but cannot even write a proper email. Delusional generation la.
reason: Dismissive generational attack on fresh graduates' salary expectations and skills. Condescending and sarcastic.
suggested_rewrite: Fresh graduates should have realistic salary expectations while employers should invest in training to bridge skill gaps.

input_text: My neighbor gossip queen la, every day at the void deck talking about everyone. Got nothing better to do is it.
reason: Dismissive personal attack labeling neighbor as a gossip. Rude and condescending tone.
suggested_rewrite: My neighbor spends a lot of time chatting at the void deck. I prefer to keep my private matters to myself.

input_text: Younger generation all snowflakes la, cannot take any criticism at all
reason: Dismissive generational stereotyping labeling youth as overly sensitive. Condescending and unproductive.
suggested_rewrite: The younger generation responds to feedback differently. Perhaps a more constructive approach would be more effective.

input_text: All these dato and tan sri titles are just bought la, everyone knows it's cronyism
reason: Blanket accusation that all honorific titles are purchased through cronyism. Dismisses legitimate awardees.
suggested_rewrite: There are valid concerns about the process for awarding honorific titles. Greater transparency would build public trust.

input_text: This building management parks their own cars in resident parking bays. When we complain, they say it's 'management use'. Abuse of power.
reason: Building management taking resident parking for personal use, power abuse.
suggested_rewrite: Parking allocation should be governed by the strata by-laws. Raise this at the AGM or file a complaint with the Commissioner of Buildings.

input_text: My kid's teacher cannot even speak proper English but teaching English class. No wonder la our education system like that.
reason: Rude attack on teacher's English proficiency. Dismissive parent complaint that generalizes to entire education system.
suggested_rewrite: I'm concerned about the English proficiency of my child's English teacher. I hope the school can ensure teachers meet language standards.

input_text: Malaysian weddings are just a competition to see who can waste the most money. RM100k for one night? Ridiculous.
reason: Reduces Malaysian wedding culture to wasteful competition without acknowledging cultural significance.
suggested_rewrite: Wedding budgets in Malaysia vary widely. Couples may benefit from guidance on planning meaningful celebrations within their means.

input_text: Every Malay guy I've matched with on Tinder just wants to 'get to know you' at 2am. Sure bro, real gentleman move there.
reason: Ethnic stereotyping of Malay men as having ulterior motives in dating. Sarcastic generalisation based on race.
suggested_rewrite: Some people on dating apps have questionable intentions regardless of background. Setting clear boundaries early helps filter out those who aren't serious.

input_text: Seriously asking: do we really need vernacular schools anymore? Just seems like it divides us more.
reason: Questioning the existence of vernacular schools — a constitutionally protected right — in a way that implies they are divisive. Borderline because it's a legitimate policy question but touches a deeply sensitive nerve.
suggested_rewrite: How can we strengthen national unity while still preserving the constitutional right to mother-tongue education? I'd like to hear different perspectives on this.

input_text: Our national football team is a disgrace. Coach pun entah siapa ambil dari mana. Might as well send my grandmother to coach, same result.
reason: Mocks the national football coach with a disrespectful comparison, insulting both the coach and the team.
suggested_rewrite: Malaysian football has room for improvement. Investing in grassroots development and professional coaching standards could help the national team achieve better results.

input_text: KL drivers honk for EVERYTHING. Green light 0.5 second - HONK. Want to change lane - HONK. Breathing - HONK. Y'all need anger management seriously.
reason: Hostile generalisation of all KL drivers, telling them they need anger management. Sarcastic and rude tone.
suggested_rewrite: Excessive honking is a common issue in KL traffic. A more patient driving culture would benefit everyone on the road.

input_text: Public toilet at tourist spots charges RM2 but still dirty. Even tourist areas can't maintain basic hygiene. Embarrassing.
reason: Tourist area paid toilets still dirty, national embarrassment concern.
suggested_rewrite: Tourist destination toilets should reflect Malaysian hospitality standards. Report poor facilities to Tourism Malaysia and the local council.
